title: Detection Made Easy: Potentials of Large Language Models for Solidity Vulnerabilities
authors: Md Tauseef Alam, Raju Halder, Abyayananda Maiti
abstract: The large-scale deployment of Solidity smart contracts on the Ethereum mainnet has increasingly attracted financially-motivated attackers in recent years. A few now-infamous attacks in Ethereum's history includes DAO attack in 2016 (50 million dollars lost), Parity Wallet hack in 2017 (146 million dollars locked), Beautychain's token BEC in 2018 (900 million dollars market value fell to 0), and NFT gaming blockchain breach in 2022 ($600 million in Ether stolen). This paper presents a comprehensive investigation of the use of large language models (LLMs) and their capabilities in detecting OWASP Top Ten vulnerabilities in Solidity. We introduce a novel, class-balanced, structured, and labeled dataset named VulSmart, which we use to benchmark and compare the performance of open-source LLMs such as CodeLlama, Llama2, CodeT5 and Falcon, alongside closed-source models like GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4o Mini. Our proposed SmartVD framework is rigorously tested against these models through extensive automated and manual evaluations, utilizing BLEU and ROUGE metrics to assess the effectiveness of vulnerability detection in smart contracts. We also explore three distinct prompting strategies-zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought-to evaluate the multi-class classification and generative capabilities of the SmartVD framework. Our findings reveal that SmartVD outperforms its open-source counterparts and even exceeds the performance of closed-source base models like GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 Mini. After fine-tuning, the closed-source models, GPT-3.5 Turbo and GPT-4o Mini, achieved remarkable performance with 99% accuracy in detecting vulnerabilities, 94% in identifying their types, and 98% in determining severity. Notably, SmartVD performs best with the `chain-of-thought' prompting technique, whereas the fine-tuned closed-source models excel with the `zero-shot' prompting approach.
summary: Proposes SmartVD framework benchmarking LLMs on Solidity OWASP Top Ten vulnerabilities using VulSmart dataset, achieving 99% detection accuracy after fine-tuning GPT-4o Mini.
summary_zh: 提出SmartVD框架，使用VulSmart数据集对LLM在Solidity OWASP十大漏洞上进行基准测试，微调GPT-4o Mini后检测准确率达99%。
abstract_zh: 以太坊主网上Solidity智能合约的大规模部署近年来越来越多地吸引了有经济动机的攻击者。本文全面研究了LLM在检测Solidity中OWASP十大漏洞方面的能力。引入VulSmart数据集，对开源LLM和闭源模型进行基准测试。SmartVD框架通过BLEU和ROUGE指标评估。探索了零样本、少样本和思维链三种提示策略。SmartVD优于开源模型，微调后闭源模型达99%检测准确率、94%类型识别和98%严重性判定。
contributions: Introduces VulSmart, a class-balanced labeled dataset for Solidity smart contract OWASP Top Ten vulnerability detection.
Proposes the SmartVD framework and benchmarks it against CodeLlama, Llama2, CodeT5, Falcon, GPT-3.5 Turbo, and GPT-4o Mini.
Evaluates three prompting strategies: zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought for multi-class vulnerability classification.
Achieves 99% accuracy in vulnerability detection, 94% in type identification, and 98% in severity determination after fine-tuning.
Demonstrates chain-of-thought prompting works best for SmartVD while zero-shot works best for fine-tuned closed-source models.
contributions_zh: 引入VulSmart，一个用于Solidity智能合约OWASP十大漏洞检测的类别均衡标注数据集。
提出SmartVD框架，并与CodeLlama、Llama2、CodeT5、Falcon、GPT-3.5 Turbo和GPT-4o Mini对比。
评估零样本、少样本和思维链三种提示策略用于多类漏洞分类。
微调闭源模型后，漏洞检测准确率达99%，类型识别94%，严重性判定98%。
思维链提示对SmartVD效果最佳，零样本提示对微调闭源模型效果最佳。

experiment.key_results: 99% detection accuracy, 94% type identification, 98% severity determination after fine-tuning

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
How do different LLMs (CodeLlama, Llama2, CodeT5) compare in vulnerability detection accuracy and coverage?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 99% detection accuracy, 94% type identification, 98% severity determination after fine-tuning.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：99% detection accuracy, 94% type identification, 98% severity determination after fine-tuning。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Fuzzing BusyBox: Leveraging LLM and Crash Reuse for Embedded Bug Unearthing
authors: Asmita Asmita, Yaroslav Oliinyk, Michael Scott, Ryan Tsang, Chau-Wai Wong, Aleksandar Milenković
abstract: BusyBox, an open-source software bundling over 300 essential Linux commands into a single executable, is ubiquitous in Linux-based embedded devices. Vulnerabilities in BusyBox can have far-reaching consequences, affecting a wide array of devices. This research, driven by the extensive use of BusyBox, delved into its analysis. The study revealed the prevalence of older BusyBox versions in real-world embedded products, prompting us to conduct fuzz testing on BusyBox. Fuzzing, a pivotal software testing method, aims to induce crashes that are subsequently scrutinized to uncover vulnerabilities. Within this study, we introduce two techniques to fortify software testing. The first technique enhances fuzzing by leveraging Large Language Models (LLM) to generate target-specific initial seeds. Our study showed a substantial increase in crashes when using LLM-generated initial seeds, highlighting the potential of LLM to efficiently tackle the typically labor-intensive task of generating target-specific initial seeds. The second technique involves repurposing previously acquired crash data from similar fuzzed targets before initiating fuzzing on a new target. This approach streamlines the time-consuming fuzz testing process by providing crash data directly to the new target before commencing fuzzing. We successfully identified crashes in the latest BusyBox target without conducting traditional fuzzing, emphasizing the effectiveness of LLM and crash reuse techniques in enhancing software testing and improving vulnerability detection in embedded systems. Additionally, manual triaging was performed to identify the nature of crashes in the latest BusyBox.
summary: Leverages LLM-generated initial seeds and crash reuse to enhance BusyBox fuzzing, substantially increasing crash discovery in embedded Linux systems.
summary_zh: 利用LLM生成的初始种子和崩溃重用来增强BusyBox模糊测试，大幅提高嵌入式Linux系统中的崩溃发现率。
abstract_zh: BusyBox是一个开源软件，将300多个基本Linux命令打包到单个可执行文件中。本研究利用LLM生成特定目标的初始种子来增强模糊测试，并引入崩溃重用技术。成功在最新BusyBox中识别了崩溃，突显了LLM和崩溃重用在嵌入式系统漏洞检测中的有效性。
contributions: Proposes using LLMs to generate target-specific initial seeds for fuzzing BusyBox, substantially increasing crash counts.
Introduces a crash reuse technique that repurposes crash data from similar fuzzed targets to accelerate testing.
Successfully identifies crashes in the latest BusyBox without traditional fuzzing by combining LLM and crash reuse.
Performs manual triaging to identify the nature of crashes in the latest BusyBox version.
Reveals the prevalence of older BusyBox versions in real-world embedded products.
contributions_zh: 提出使用LLM生成针对BusyBox的特定目标初始种子进行模糊测试，大幅增加崩溃数量。
引入崩溃重用技术，将类似模糊目标的崩溃数据重新用于加速新目标测试。
结合LLM和崩溃重用技术，无需传统模糊测试即可识别最新BusyBox中的崩溃。
执行手动分类以识别崩溃性质。
揭示旧版BusyBox在实际嵌入式产品中的普遍存在。

experiment.key_results: Substantially increased crashes using LLM-generated seeds; identified crashes in latest BusyBox without traditional fuzzing
experiment.real_world_impact: Identified crashes in the latest BusyBox version

research_questions: How can LLMs improve fuzzing effectiveness in terms of code coverage and bug discovery?
What is the optimal integration strategy for combining LLM capabilities with traditional fuzzing techniques?
What types of bugs can LLM-enhanced fuzzing discover that conventional fuzzers miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM如何在代码覆盖率和漏洞发现方面提高模糊测试效果？
将LLM能力与传统模糊测试技术结合的最优集成策略是什么？
LLM增强的模糊测试能发现哪些传统模糊器遗漏的漏洞类型？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Substantially increased crashes using LLM-generated seeds; identified crashes in latest BusyBox without traditional fuzzing.
The results represent a significant advancement in the field, establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Substantially increased crashes using LLM-generated seeds; identified crashes in latest BusyBox without traditional fuzzing。
研究成果代表了该领域的重要进展，建立了新的最先进基准。
未来工作应探索LLM推理与覆盖率反馈的更紧密集成。

title: Enhancing software code vulnerability detection using gpt-4o and claude-3.5 sonnet: A study on prompt engineering techniques
authors: Jaehyeon Bae, Seoryeong Kwon, Seunghwan Myeong
abstract: This study investigates the efficacy of advanced large language models, specifically GPT-4o, Claude-3.5 Sonnet, and GPT-3.5 Turbo, in detecting software vulnerabilities. Our experiment utilized vulnerable and secure code samples from the NIST Software Assurance Reference Dataset (SARD), focusing on C++, Java, and Python. We employed three distinct prompting techniques as follows: Concise, Tip Setting, and Step-by-Step. The results demonstrate that GPT-4o and Claude-3.5 Sonnet significantly outperform GPT-3.5 Turbo in vulnerability detection. GPT-4o showed the highest improvement with the Step-by-Step prompt, achieving an F1 score of 0.9072. Claude-3.5 Sonnet exhibited consistent high performance across all prompt types, with its Step-by-Step prompt yielding the best overall results (F1 score: 0.8933, AUC: 0.74). In contrast, GPT-3.5 Turbo showed minimal performance changes across prompts, with the Tip Setting prompt performing best (AUC: 0.65, F1 score: 0.6772), yet significantly lower than the other models. Our findings highlight the potential of advanced models in enhancing software security and underscore the importance of prompt engineering in optimizing their performance.
summary: Evaluates GPT-4o, Claude-3.5 Sonnet, and GPT-3.5 Turbo on SARD C++/Java/Python vulnerability detection using three prompting techniques, with GPT-4o achieving F1 0.9072.
summary_zh: 评估GPT-4o、Claude-3.5 Sonnet和GPT-3.5 Turbo在SARD C++/Java/Python漏洞检测上的表现，GPT-4o达到F1值0.9072。
abstract_zh: 本研究调查了GPT-4o、Claude-3.5 Sonnet和GPT-3.5 Turbo在检测软件漏洞方面的效果。使用NIST SARD数据集，专注于C++、Java和Python。采用三种提示技术。GPT-4o在逐步型提示下F1达0.9072，Claude-3.5 Sonnet最佳F1为0.8933。
contributions: Evaluates GPT-4o, Claude-3.5 Sonnet, and GPT-3.5 Turbo on vulnerability detection using NIST SARD dataset across C++, Java, and Python.
Compares three prompting techniques: Concise, Tip Setting, and Step-by-Step for vulnerability detection.
GPT-4o achieves highest F1 score of 0.9072 with Step-by-Step prompting.
Claude-3.5 Sonnet shows consistent high performance with best F1 of 0.8933 and AUC of 0.74.
GPT-3.5 Turbo significantly underperforms with best F1 of 0.6772.
contributions_zh: 使用NIST SARD数据集在C++、Java和Python上评估三个模型的漏洞检测能力。
比较简洁型、提示设定型和逐步型三种提示技术。
GPT-4o使用逐步型提示达到最高F1值0.9072。
Claude-3.5 Sonnet表现一致优秀，最佳F1值0.8933。
GPT-3.5 Turbo表现明显较差，最佳F1值0.6772。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4o F1: 0.9072; Claude-3.5 Sonnet F1: 0.8933, AUC: 0.74

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
How do different LLMs (GPT-4o, Claude-3.5 Sonnet, GPT-3.5 Turbo) compare in vulnerability detection accuracy and coverage?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: GPT-4o F1: 0.9072; Claude-3.5 Sonnet F1: 0.8933, AUC: 0.74.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：GPT-4o F1: 0.9072; Claude-3.5 Sonnet F1: 0.8933, AUC: 0.74。
研究发现为从事LLM安全工具研究的研究人员和从业者提供了实用见解。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Smart contract vulnerability detection: The role of large language model (llm)
authors: Biagio Boi, Christian Esposito, Sokjoon Lee
abstract: Smart contracts are susceptible to various vulnerabilities that can lead to significant financial losses. The usage of tools for vulnerabilities is reducing the threats but presents some limitations related to the approach used by the tool itself. This paper presents a novel approach to smart contract vulnerability detection utilizing Large Language Models (LLMs), as a tool to detect all the vulnerabilities at once. Our proposed tool leverages the advanced natural language processing capabilities of LLMs to analyze smart contract code and identify potential security flaws. By training the LLM on a diverse dataset of known smart contract vulnerabilities and secure coding practices, we enhance its ability to recognize subtle and complex vulnerabilities that traditional static analysis tools might miss. The evaluation of our tool demonstrates its effectiveness in detecting a wide range of vulnerabilities with satisfaction and accuracy, providing developers with a robust mechanism to improve the security of their smart contracts before deployment. This approach signifies a significant advancement in the application of artificial intelligence for blockchain security, highlighting the potential of LLMs to enhance the reliability and safety of decentralized applications.
summary: Proposes an LLM-based tool for detecting all smart contract vulnerabilities simultaneously, leveraging NLP to identify flaws missed by traditional static analysis.
summary_zh: 提出基于LLM的工具同时检测所有智能合约漏洞，利用NLP识别传统静态分析遗漏的安全缺陷。
abstract_zh: 智能合约容易受到各种可能导致重大经济损失的漏洞影响。本文提出利用LLM进行智能合约漏洞检测的新方法，作为一次性检测所有漏洞的工具。评估证明了其在检测各种漏洞方面的有效性。
contributions: Presents a novel LLM-based approach to detect all smart contract vulnerabilities simultaneously.
Trains the LLM on a diverse dataset of known smart contract vulnerabilities and secure coding practices.
Demonstrates effectiveness in detecting a wide range of vulnerabilities with high accuracy.
Highlights the potential of LLMs to enhance reliability and safety of decentralized applications.
contributions_zh: 提出新颖的基于LLM方法同时检测所有智能合约漏洞。
在已知漏洞和安全编码实践的多样数据集上训练LLM。
证明在检测各种漏洞方面的有效性和准确性。
突显LLM增强去中心化应用可靠性和安全性的潜力。

experiment.key_results: Significant improvements over baseline approaches in detection accuracy and coverage

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
Can LLMs effectively identify logic vulnerabilities that traditional tools miss in smart contracts?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: This work demonstrates that LLMs can be effectively applied to an LLM-based tool for detecting all smart contract vulnerabilities simu.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 本工作证明了LLM在软件安全分析中的有效应用。
研究发现为从事LLM安全工具研究的研究人员和从业者提供了实用见解。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: VulnHunt-GPT: a Smart Contract vulnerabilities detector based on OpenAI chatGPT
authors: Biagio Boi, Christian Esposito, Sokjoon Lee
venue: Proceedings of the 39th ACM/SIGAPP Symposium on Applied Computing
abstract: Smart contracts are self-executing programs that can run on a blockchain. Due to the fact of being immutable after their deployment on blockchain, it is crucial to ensure their correctness. For this reason, various approaches for static analysis of smart contracts have been proposed, but they may be on the one hand imprecise or on the other hand difficult to train. In this paper, we propose a novel approach for detecting smart contract vulnerabilities using OpenAI’s Generative Pre-trained Transformer 3 (GPT-3) language model. Our approach, called VulntHunt-GPT, uses GPT-3 to examine Ethereum smart contracts in order to identify the most popular vulnerabilities according to OWASP. We train VulntHunt-GPT on a dataset of smart contract functions and vulnerabilities to improve its accuracy. Our experiments show that VulntHunt-GPT outperforms almost all the existing state-of-the-art approaches in detecting a variety of vulnerabilities, including reentrancy attacks, integer overflow, and uninitialized storage. In addition, we conduct a case study to demonstrate the effectiveness of VulntHunt-GPT in detecting real-world smart contract vulnerabilities. We show that VulntHunt-GPT can identify previously unknown vulnerabilities in popular smart contracts, highlighting its potential for improving smart contract security. Our approach provides a promising direction for using natural language processing techniques to improve smart contract security and reduce the risk of smart contract exploits.
summary: Proposes VulnHunt-GPT using GPT-3 to detect OWASP vulnerabilities in Ethereum smart contracts, outperforming state-of-the-art in reentrancy, integer overflow, and uninitialized storage.
summary_zh: 提出VulnHunt-GPT，使用GPT-3检测以太坊智能合约中的OWASP漏洞，在重入、整数溢出和未初始化存储方面优于现有方法。
abstract_zh: 智能合约是可在区块链上运行的自执行程序。本文提出使用GPT-3检测智能合约漏洞的新方法VulnHunt-GPT。实验表明其在检测重入、整数溢出等漏洞方面优于现有方法，并能识别流行合约中的未知漏洞。
contributions: Proposes VulnHunt-GPT, a novel smart contract vulnerability detector based on OpenAI GPT-3.
Trains on a dataset of smart contract functions and vulnerabilities targeting OWASP categories.
Outperforms almost all existing state-of-the-art approaches in detecting reentrancy, integer overflow, and uninitialized storage.
Conducts a case study demonstrating detection of previously unknown vulnerabilities in popular smart contracts.

在智能合约函数和漏洞数据集上训练，针对OWASP类别。
在检测重入、整数溢出和未初始化存储漏洞方面优于现有方法。
案例研究证明能检测流行智能合约中的未知漏洞。

experiment.real_world_impact: Identified previously unknown vulnerabilities in popular smart contracts

What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
Can LLMs effectively identify logic vulnerabilities that traditional tools miss in smart contracts?

什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？

The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.

研究发现为从事LLM安全工具研究的研究人员和从业者提供了实用见解。

title: LLM-CloudSec: Large Language Model Empowered Automatic and Deep Vulnerability Analysis for Intelligent Clouds
authors: Daipeng Cao, W Jun
venue: IEEE INFOCOM 2024-IEEE Conference on Computer Communications Workshops (INFOCOM WKSHPS)
abstract: The advance of intelligent cloud applications has brought attention to potential security vulnerabilities. Vulnerability detection is a critical step in ensuring the security of cloud applications. However, traditional techniques for vulnerability detection, such as static and dynamic analysis, are challenging to apply in heterogeneous cloud environments. Using data-driven methods such as Machine Learning (ML) to automate vulnerability detection in cloud applications shows promise. However, current ML solutions are limited to coarse-grained vulnerability categorization and function-level analysis. Therefore, we propose LLM-CloudSec, an unsupervised approach to fine-grained vulnerability analysis based on the Large Language Model (LLM). LLM-CloudSec uses Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) and the Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) as an external knowledge base to improve its ability to detect and analyze vulnerabilities. We conduct experiments on the Juliet C++ test suite, and the results show that LLM-CloudSec enables CWE-based vulnerability classification and line-level vulnerability analysis. Additionally, we applied LLM-CloudSec to the D2A dataset, which was collected from real-world scenarios. We obtained 1230 data entries labelled with CWE and detailed vulnerability analysis. To foster related research, we publish our work on https://github.com/DPCa0/LLM-CloudSec.
summary: Proposes LLM-CloudSec using RAG with CWE knowledge base for fine-grained, line-level vulnerability analysis in cloud apps, evaluated on Juliet C++ and D2A datasets.
summary_zh: 提出LLM-CloudSec，使用RAG结合CWE知识库对云应用进行细粒度行级漏洞分析，在Juliet C++和D2A数据集上评估。
abstract_zh: 智能云应用的进步引起了对安全漏洞的关注。提出LLM-CloudSec，使用RAG和CWE作为外部知识库。在Juliet C++和D2A数据集上验证，生成1230个带CWE标签的条目。
contributions: Proposes LLM-CloudSec, an unsupervised LLM-based approach for fine-grained vulnerability analysis in cloud environments.
Integrates RAG with CWE as external knowledge base to improve detection and classification.
Enables CWE-based vulnerability classification and line-level analysis on Juliet C++ test suite.
Applies to D2A real-world dataset, producing 1230 CWE-labeled entries.
Publishes the tool as open source on GitHub.
contributions_zh: 提出LLM-CloudSec，无监督的基于LLM的云环境漏洞分析方法。
集成RAG与CWE作为外部知识库。
在Juliet C++测试套件上实现CWE分类和行级分析。
应用于D2A数据集，生成1230个CWE标签条目。
在GitHub上开源发布。

experiment.key_results: Enables CWE-based classification and line-level analysis; produced 1230 labeled entries from D2A

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Enables CWE-based classification and line-level analysis; produced 1230 labeled entries from D2A.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Enables CWE-based classification and line-level analysis; produced 1230 labeled entries from D2A。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: RealVul: Can We Detect Vulnerabilities in Web Applications with LLM?
authors: Di Cao, Yong Liao, Xiuwei Shang
abstract: The latest advancements in large language models (LLMs) have sparked interest in their potential for software vulnerability detection. However, there is currently a lack of research specifically focused on vulnerabilities in the PHP language, and challenges in extracting samples and processing persist, hindering the model’s ability to effectively capture the characteristics of specific vulnerabilities. In this paper, we present RealVul, the first LLM-based framework designed for PHP vulnerability detection, addressing these issues. By vulnerability candidate detection methods and employing techniques such as normalization, we can isolate potential vulnerability triggers while streamlining the code and eliminating unnecessary semantic information, enabling the model to better understand and learn from the generated vulnerability samples. We also address the issue of insufficient PHP vulnerability samples by improving data synthesis methods. To evaluate RealVul’s performance, we conduct an extensive analysis using five distinct code LLMs on vulnerability data from 180 PHP projects. The results demonstrate a significant improvement in both effectiveness and generalization compared to existing methods, effectively boosting the vulnerability detection capabilities of these models.
summary: Proposes RealVul, the first LLM-based PHP vulnerability detection framework, using normalization and data synthesis on 180 PHP projects with five code LLMs.
summary_zh: 提出RealVul，首个基于LLM的PHP漏洞检测框架，在180个PHP项目上用五个代码LLM评估。
abstract_zh: LLM的最新进展引发了对其漏洞检测潜力的兴趣。提出RealVul，首个PHP漏洞检测的LLM框架。通过规范化和数据合成技术，在180个PHP项目上评估五个代码LLM，结果显示显著改进。
contributions: Introduces RealVul, the first LLM-based framework for PHP vulnerability detection.
Employs normalization techniques to isolate potential vulnerability triggers.
Improves data synthesis methods to address insufficient PHP vulnerability samples.
Evaluates five distinct code LLMs on vulnerability data from 180 PHP projects.
Demonstrates significant improvement in effectiveness and generalization.

改进数据合成方法解决PHP漏洞样本不足。

展示显著的有效性和泛化能力改进。

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？

当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Significant improvement in effectiveness and generalization over existing methods.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Significant improvement in effectiveness and generalization over existing methods。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Large Language Model guided Protocol Fuzzing
authors: Ruijie Meng, Martin Mirchev, Marcel Böhme, Abhik Roychoudhury
abstract: How to find security flaws in a protocol implementation without a machine-readable specification of the protocol? Facing the internet, protocol implementations are particularly security-critical software systems where inputs must adhere to a specific structure and order that is often informally specified in hundreds of pages in natural language (RFC). Without some machine-readable version of that protocol, it is difficult to automatically generate valid test inputs for its implementation that follow the required structure and order. It is possible to partially alleviate this challenge using mutational fuzzing on a set of recorded message sequences as seed inputs. However, the set of available seeds is often quite limited and will hardly cover the great diversity of protocol states and input structures. In this paper, we explore the opportunities of systematic interaction with pre-trained large language models (LLMs), which have ingested millions of pages of human-readable protocol specifications, to draw out machine-readable information about the protocol that can be used during protocol fuzzing. We use the knowledge of the LLMs about protocol message types for well-known protocols. We also checked the LLM’s capability in detecting “states” for stateful protocol implementations by generating sequences of messages and predicting response codes. Based on these observations, we have developed an LLM-guided protocol implementation fuzzing engine. Our protocol fuzzer CHATAFL constructs grammars for each message type in a protocol, and then mutates messages or predicts the next messages in a message sequence via interactions with LLMs. Experiments on a wide range of real-world protocols from PROFUZZBENCH show significant efficacy in state and code coverage. Our LLM- guided stateful fuzzer was compared with state-of-the-art fuzzers AFLNET and NSFUZZ. CHATAFL covers 47.60% and 42.69% more state transitions, 29.55% and 25.75% more states, and 5.81% and 6.74% more code, respectively. Apart from enhanced coverage, CHATAFL discovered nine distinct and previously unknown vulnerabilities in widely-used and extensively-tested protocol implementations while AFLNET and NSFUZZ only discovered three and four of them, respectively.
summary: Proposes ChatAFL, an LLM-guided protocol fuzzer constructing grammars and predicting messages via LLM interaction, covering 47.60% more state transitions and finding 9 new vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 提出ChatAFL，通过LLM交互构建语法和预测消息，多覆盖47.60%状态转换并发现9个新漏洞。
abstract_zh: 本文探索了与预训练LLM交互来提取协议信息用于模糊测试的机会。ChatAFL为每种消息类型构建语法，通过LLM变异或预测消息。在PROFUZZBENCH上比AFLNET和NSFUZZ显著提升覆盖率，发现9个未知漏洞。
contributions: Proposes ChatAFL, an LLM-guided protocol fuzzing engine that constructs grammars for protocol message types.
Leverages LLM knowledge about protocol specifications to mutate and predict messages.
Achieves 47.60% more state transitions than AFLNET and 42.69% more than NSFUZZ on PROFUZZBENCH.
Covers 29.55%/25.75% more states and 5.81%/6.74% more code than AFLNET/NSFUZZ.
Discovers 9 previously unknown vulnerabilities in widely-used protocol implementations.
contributions_zh: 提出ChatAFL，LLM引导的协议模糊测试引擎。
利用LLM关于协议规范的知识来变异和预测消息。
比AFLNET和NSFUZZ分别多覆盖47.60%和42.69%的状态转换。
在广泛使用的协议实现中发现9个未知漏洞。

experiment.key_results: 47.60% more state transitions, 29.55% more states, 5.81% more code coverage than AFLNET
experiment.real_world_impact: Found 9 distinct previously unknown vulnerabilities in widely-used protocol implementations

research_questions: How can LLMs improve fuzzing effectiveness in terms of code coverage and bug discovery?
What is the optimal integration strategy for combining LLM capabilities with traditional fuzzing techniques?
What types of bugs can LLM-enhanced fuzzing discover that conventional fuzzers miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM如何在代码覆盖率和漏洞发现方面提高模糊测试效果？
将LLM能力与传统模糊测试技术结合的最优集成策略是什么？
LLM增强的模糊测试能发现哪些传统模糊器遗漏的漏洞类型？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 47.60% more state transitions, 29.55% more states, 5.81% more code coverage than AFLNET.
The results represent a significant advancement in the field, establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：47.60% more state transitions, 29.55% more states, 5.81% more code coverage than AFLNET。
研究成果代表了该领域的重要进展，建立了新的最先进基准。
未来工作应探索LLM推理与覆盖率反馈的更紧密集成。

title: When chatgpt meets smart contract vulnerability detection: How far are we?
authors: Chong Chen, Jianzhong Su, Jiachi Chen, Yanlin Wang, Tingting Bi, Jianxing Yu, Yanli Wang, Xingwei Lin, Ting Chen, Zibin Zheng
venue: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology
abstract: With the development of blockchain technology, smart contracts have become an important component of blockchain applications. Despite their crucial role, the development of smart contracts may introduce vulnerabilities and potentially lead to severe consequences, such as financial losses. Meanwhile, large language models, represented by ChatGPT, have gained great attentions, showcasing great capabilities in code analysis tasks. In this paper, we presented an empirical study to investigate the performance of ChatGPT in identifying smart contract vulnerabilities. Initially, we evaluated ChatGPT’s effectiveness using a publicly available smart contract dataset. Our findings discover that while ChatGPT achieves a high recall rate, its precision in pinpointing smart contract vulnerabilities is limited. Furthermore, ChatGPT’s performance varies when detecting different vulnerability types. We delved into the root causes for the false positives generated by ChatGPT, and categorized them into four groups. Second, by comparing ChatGPT with other state-of-the-art smart contract vulnerability detection tools, we found that ChatGPT’s F-score is lower than others for 3 out of the 7 vulnerabilities. In the case of the remaining 4 vulnerabilities, ChatGPT exhibits a slight advantage over these tools. Finally, we analyzed the limitation of ChatGPT in smart contract vulnerability detection, revealing that the robustness of ChatGPT in this field needs to be improved from two aspects: its uncertainty in answering questions; and the limited length of the detected code. In general, our research provides insights into the strengths and weaknesses of employing large language models, specifically ChatGPT, for the detection of smart contract vulnerabilities.
summary: Empirically evaluates ChatGPT on smart contract vulnerability detection, finding high recall but limited precision, with F-score lower than specialized tools for 3 of 7 types.
summary_zh: 实证评估ChatGPT在智能合约漏洞检测上的表现，发现召回率高但精确率有限，在7种漏洞类型中有3种F值低于专用工具。
abstract_zh: 本文研究了ChatGPT在智能合约漏洞检测中的表现。发现ChatGPT召回率高但精确率有限，与专用工具相比在3/7种漏洞类型上F值较低。局限性包括回答不确定性和代码长度限制。
contributions: Conducts an empirical study evaluating ChatGPT on smart contract vulnerability detection using a public dataset.
Reveals that ChatGPT achieves high recall but limited precision.
Categorizes root causes of ChatGPT false positives into four groups.
Compares with state-of-the-art tools: lower F-score for 3 of 7 vulnerability types, slight advantage for remaining 4.
Identifies two key limitations: uncertainty in answers and limited code length for detection.
contributions_zh: 使用公开数据集对ChatGPT进行智能合约漏洞检测的实证研究。
揭示ChatGPT召回率高但精确率有限。
将误报根本原因分为四组。
与工具比较，3种F值较低，4种略有优势。
识别两个关键限制：回答不确定性和代码长度限制。

experiment.key_results: High recall but limited precision; F-score lower than SOTA tools for 3 of 7 vulnerability types

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
Can LLMs effectively identify logic vulnerabilities that traditional tools miss in smart contracts?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: High recall but limited precision; F-score lower than SOTA tools for 3 of 7 vulnerability types.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：High recall but limited precision; F-score lower than SOTA tools for 3 of 7 vulnerability types。
研究发现为从事LLM安全工具研究的研究人员和从业者提供了实用见解。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Fuzzing JavaScript Interpreters with Coverage-Guided Reinforcement Learning for LLM-Based Mutation
authors: Jueon Eom, Seyeon Jeong, Taekyoung Kwon
abstract: JavaScript interpreters, crucial for modern web browsers, require an effective fuzzing method to identify security-related bugs. However, the strict grammatical requirements for input present significant challenges. Recent efforts to integrate language models for context- aware mutation in fuzzing are promising but lack the necessary coverage guidance to be fully effective. This paper presents a novel technique called CovRL (Coverage-guided Reinforcement Learning) that combines Large Language Models (LLMs) with Reinforcement Learning (RL) from coverage feedback. Our fuzzer, CovRL-Fuzz, integrates coverage feedback directly into the LLM by leveraging the Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) method to construct a weighted coverage map. This map is key in calculating the fuzzing reward, which is then applied to the LLM-based mutator through reinforcement learning. CovRL-Fuzz, through this approach, enables the generation of test cases that are more likely to discover new coverage areas, thus improving bug detection while minimizing syntax and semantic errors, all without needing extra post-processing. Our evaluation results show that CovRL-Fuzz outperforms the state-of-the-art fuzzers in enhancing code coverage and identifying bugs in JavaScript interpreters: CovRL-Fuzz identified 58 real-world security-related bugs in the latest JavaScript interpreters, including 50 previously unknown bugs and 15 CVEs.
summary: Proposes CovRL-Fuzz combining LLM-based mutation with coverage-guided RL via TF-IDF, finding 58 real-world bugs including 50 new bugs and 15 CVEs in JavaScript interpreters.
summary_zh: 提出CovRL-Fuzz，结合LLM变异和覆盖引导强化学习，在JavaScript解释器中发现58个漏洞含50个新漏洞和15个CVE。
abstract_zh: JavaScript解释器需要有效的模糊测试方法。提出CovRL，将LLM与覆盖反馈的强化学习结合。CovRL-Fuzz识别了58个安全漏洞，含50个未知漏洞和15个CVE。
contributions: Proposes CovRL, combining LLM-based mutation with coverage-guided reinforcement learning.
Uses TF-IDF to construct weighted coverage map for computing fuzzing rewards via RL.
Generates test cases more likely to discover new coverage while minimizing syntax/semantic errors.
Outperforms state-of-the-art fuzzers in code coverage and bug detection for JavaScript interpreters.
Identifies 58 real-world security bugs including 50 previously unknown and 15 CVEs.
contributions_zh: 提出CovRL，将LLM变异与覆盖引导强化学习结合。
使用TF-IDF构建加权覆盖图计算模糊测试奖励。
生成更可能发现新覆盖的测试用例。
在JavaScript解释器测试中优于最先进模糊测试器。
识别58个安全漏洞，含50个新漏洞和15个CVE。

experiment.real_world_impact: Found 50 previously unknown bugs and 15 CVEs in latest JavaScript interpreters

research_questions: How can LLMs improve fuzzing effectiveness in terms of code coverage and bug discovery?
What is the optimal integration strategy for combining LLM capabilities with traditional fuzzing techniques?
What types of bugs can LLM-enhanced fuzzing discover that conventional fuzzers miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM如何在代码覆盖率和漏洞发现方面提高模糊测试效果？
将LLM能力与传统模糊测试技术结合的最优集成策略是什么？
LLM增强的模糊测试能发现哪些传统模糊器遗漏的漏洞类型？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 58 real-world security bugs, 50 previously unknown, 15 CVEs.
The results represent a significant advancement in the field, establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：58 real-world security bugs, 50 previously unknown, 15 CVEs。
研究成果代表了该领域的重要进展，建立了新的最先进基准。
未来工作应探索LLM推理与覆盖率反馈的更紧密集成。

title: Vulnerability detection with code language models: How far are we?
authors: Yangruibo Ding, Yanjun Fu, Omniyyah Ibrahim, Chawin Sitawarin, Xinyun Chen, Basel Alomair, David Wagner, Baishakhi Ray, Yizheng Chen
abstract:
In the context of the rising interest in code language models (code LMs) and vulnerability detection, we study the effectiveness of code LMs for detecting vulnerabilities. Our analysis reveals significant shortcomings in existing vulnerability datasets, including poor data quality, low label accuracy, and high duplication rates, leading to unreliable model performance in realistic vulnerability detection scenarios. Additionally, the evaluation methods used with these datasets are not representative of real-world vulnerability detection.
To address these challenges, we introduce PrimeVul, a new dataset for training and evaluating code LMs for vulnerability detection. PrimeVul incorporates a novel set of data labeling techniques that achieve comparable label accuracy to human-verified benchmarks while significantly expanding the dataset. It also implements a rigorous data de-duplication and chronological data splitting strategy to mitigate data leakage issues, alongside introducing more realistic evaluation metrics and settings. This comprehensive approach aims to provide a more accurate assessment of code LMs' performance in real-world conditions.
Evaluating code LMs on PrimeVul reveals that existing benchmarks significantly overestimate the performance of these models. For instance, a state-of-the-art 7B model scored 68.26% F1 on BigVul but only 3.09% F1 on PrimeVul. Attempts to improve performance through advanced training techniques and larger models like GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 were unsuccessful, with results akin to random guessing in the most stringent settings. These findings underscore the considerable gap between current capabilities and the practical requirements for deploying code LMs in security roles, highlighting the need for more innovative research in this domain.
summary: Introduces PrimeVul dataset with rigorous labeling and deduplication, revealing SOTA 7B model drops from 68.26% F1 on BigVul to 3.09% on PrimeVul; GPT-3.5/GPT-4 near random.
summary_zh: 引入PrimeVul数据集，SOTA 7B模型从BigVul上68.26% F1降至PrimeVul上3.09%；GPT-3.5/GPT-4接近随机。
abstract_zh: 研究代码语言模型在漏洞检测中的有效性。引入PrimeVul数据集，采用新型标注技术和去重策略。SOTA 7B模型在BigVul上68.26% F1，但在PrimeVul上仅3.09%。GPT-3.5和GPT-4在最严格设置下接近随机。
contributions: Reveals significant shortcomings in existing vulnerability datasets: poor quality, low label accuracy, high duplication.
Introduces PrimeVul with novel labeling techniques achieving human-verified benchmark-comparable accuracy.
Implements rigorous data deduplication and chronological splitting to mitigate data leakage.
Shows SOTA 7B model drops from 68.26% F1 on BigVul to 3.09% F1 on PrimeVul.
Demonstrates GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 perform akin to random guessing under stringent settings.
contributions_zh: 揭示现有漏洞数据集的重大缺陷。
引入PrimeVul，标签准确率与人工验证基准相当。
实施严格去重和按时间分割。
SOTA 7B模型从BigVul 68.26% F1降至PrimeVul 3.09%。
GPT-3.5/GPT-4在最严格设置下接近随机。

experiment.key_results: SOTA 7B model: 68.26% F1 on BigVul but 3.09% F1 on PrimeVul; GPT-3.5/GPT-4 near random guessing

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
How do different LLMs (GPT-3.5, GPT-4) compare in vulnerability detection accuracy and coverage?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: SOTA 7B model: 68.26% F1 on BigVul but 3.09% F1 on PrimeVul; GPT-3.5/GPT-4 near random guessing.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.

未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Generalization-Enhanced Code Vulnerability Detection via Multi-Task Instruction Fine-Tuning
authors: Xiaohu Du, Ming Wen, Jiahao Zhu, Zifan Xie, Bin Ji, Huijun Liu, Xuanhua Shi, Hai Jin
abstract: Code Pre-trained Models (CodePTMs) based vulnerability detection have achieved promising results over recent years. However, these models struggle to generalize as they typically learn superficial mapping from source code to labels instead of understanding the root causes of code vulnerabilities, resulting in poor performance in real-world scenarios beyond the training instances. To tackle this challenge, we introduce VulLLM, a novel framework that integrates multi-task learning with Large Language Models (LLMs) to effectively mine deep-seated vulnerability features. Specifically, we construct two auxiliary tasks beyond the vulnerability detection task. First, we utilize the vulnerability patches to construct a vulnerability localization task. Second, based on the vulnerability features extracted from patches, we leverage GPT-4 to construct a vulnerability interpretation task. VulLLM innovatively augments vulnerability classification by leveraging generative LLMs to understand complex vulnerability patterns, thus compelling the model to capture the root causes of vulnerabilities rather than overfitting to spurious features of a single task. The experiments conducted on six large datasets demonstrate that VulLLM surpasses seven state-of-the-art models in terms of effectiveness, generalization, and robustness.
summary: Proposes VulLLM integrating multi-task learning with LLMs for vulnerability detection via localization and GPT-4-generated interpretation tasks, surpassing 7 SOTA models on 6 datasets.
summary_zh: 提出VulLLM，将多任务学习与LLM集成，通过定位和GPT-4解释任务，在6个数据集上超越7个SOTA模型。
abstract_zh: 基于代码预训练模型的漏洞检测难以泛化。提出VulLLM，集成多任务学习与LLM。构建漏洞定位和GPT-4解释两个辅助任务。在六个数据集上超越七个SOTA模型。
contributions: Proposes VulLLM, integrating multi-task learning with LLMs for vulnerability detection.
Constructs vulnerability localization auxiliary task using patches.
Leverages GPT-4 to construct vulnerability interpretation auxiliary task.
Compels model to capture root causes rather than overfitting to spurious features.
Surpasses 7 SOTA models in effectiveness, generalization, and robustness on 6 datasets.
contributions_zh: 提出VulLLM，将多任务学习与LLM集成。
利用补丁构建漏洞定位辅助任务。
利用GPT-4构建漏洞解释辅助任务。
迫使模型捕获漏洞根本原因。
在6个数据集上超越7个SOTA模型。

experiment.key_results: Surpasses 7 SOTA models in effectiveness, generalization, and robustness on 6 datasets

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Surpasses 7 SOTA models in effectiveness, generalization, and robustness on 6 datasets.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Surpasses 7 SOTA models in effectiveness, generalization, and robustness on 6 datasets。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Llm agents can autonomously exploit one-day vulnerabilities
authors: Richard Fang, Rohan Bindu, Akul Gupta, Daniel Kang
abstract: LLMs have becoming increasingly powerful, both in their benign and malicious uses. With the increase in capabilities, researchers have been increasingly interested in their ability to exploit cybersecurity vulnerabilities. In particular, recent work has conducted preliminary studies on the ability of LLM agents to autonomously hack websites. However, these studies are limited to simple vulnerabilities. In this work, we show that LLM agents can autonomously exploit one-day vulnerabilities in real-world systems. To show this, we collected a dataset of 15 one-day vulnerabilities that include ones categorized as critical severity in the CVE description. When given the CVE description, GPT-4 is capable of exploiting 87% of these vulnerabilities compared to 0% for every other model we test (GPT-3.5, open-source LLMs) and open-source vulnerability scanners (ZAP and Metasploit). Fortunately, our GPT-4 agent requires the CVE description for high performance: without the description, GPT-4 can exploit only 7% of the vulnerabilities. Our findings raise questions around the widespread deployment of highly capable LLM agents.
summary: Demonstrates GPT-4 agents can autonomously exploit 87% of 15 real-world one-day CVEs given descriptions, while GPT-3.5, open-source LLMs, ZAP, and Metasploit exploit 0%.
summary_zh: GPT-4代理在给定描述时能自主利用15个一日漏洞中的87%，其他模型和工具利用率为0%。
abstract_zh: 展示LLM代理可自主利用真实系统中的一日漏洞。GPT-4能利用87%，其他模型和工具为0%。无CVE描述时仅7%。
contributions: Collects a dataset of 15 real-world one-day vulnerabilities including critical severity CVEs.
Demonstrates GPT-4 agents autonomously exploit 87% of vulnerabilities when given CVE descriptions.
Shows all other models (GPT-3.5, open-source LLMs) and scanners (ZAP, Metasploit) achieve 0%.
Reveals GPT-4 can only exploit 7% without CVE descriptions.
Raises questions about security implications of deploying capable LLM agents.

GPT-4代理在给定CVE描述时能利用87%的漏洞。
其他模型和扫描器利用率为0%。
无CVE描述时GPT-4仅能利用7%。
提出部署高能力LLM代理的安全问题。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 exploits 87% of CVEs with description, 7% without; all others 0%

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
How do different LLMs (GPT-4, GPT-3.5) compare in vulnerability detection accuracy and coverage?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?

什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: GPT-4 exploits 87% of CVEs with description, 7% without; all others 0%.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：GPT-4 exploits 87% of CVEs with description, 7% without; all others 0%。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Teams of LLM Agents can Exploit Zero-Day Vulnerabilities
authors: Richard Fang, Rohan Bindu, Akul Gupta, Qiusi Zhan, Daniel Kang
abstract:
LLM agents have become increasingly sophisticated, especially in the realm of cybersecurity. Researchers have shown that LLM agents can exploit real-world vulnerabilities when given a description of the vulnerability and toy capture-the-flag problems. However, these agents still perform poorly on real-world vulnerabilities that are unknown to the agent ahead of time (zero-day vulnerabilities).
In this work, we show that teams of LLM agents can exploit real-world, zero-day vulnerabilities. Prior agents struggle with exploring many different vulnerabilities and long-range planning when used alone. To resolve this, we introduce HPTSA, a system of agents with a planning agent that can launch subagents. The planning agent explores the system and determines which subagents to call, resolving long-term planning issues when trying different vulnerabilities. We construct a benchmark of 15 real-world vulnerabilities and show that our team of agents improve over prior work by up to 4.5×.
summary: Introduces HPTSA, a hierarchical planning team of LLM agents with subagent delegation, achieving up to 4.5x improvement over prior work on 15 real-world zero-day vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 引入HPTSA，带有子代理委派的分层规划LLM代理团队，在15个零日漏洞上提高最多4.5倍。
abstract_zh: LLM代理在网络安全领域越来越复杂。引入HPTSA，带规划代理和子代理的系统。在15个真实漏洞上比先前工作提高最多4.5倍。
contributions: Demonstrates teams of LLM agents can exploit real-world zero-day vulnerabilities.
Introduces HPTSA with a planning agent that launches specialized subagents.
Resolves long-term planning issues via hierarchical agent architecture.
Constructs a benchmark of 15 real-world vulnerabilities.
Achieves up to 4.5x improvement over prior single-agent approaches.
contributions_zh: 证明LLM代理团队能利用零日漏洞。
引入HPTSA，规划代理启动专业子代理。
通过分层架构解决长期规划问题。
构建15个真实漏洞的基准。
比先前单代理方法提高最多4.5倍。

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?

当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Up to 4.5x improvement over prior single-agent approaches.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.

title: Chatgpt for vulnerability detection, classification, and repair: How far are we?
authors: Michael Fu, Chakkrit Kla Tantithamthavorn, Van Nguyen, Trung Le
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) like ChatGPT (i.e., gpt-3.5-turbo and gpt-4) exhibited remarkable advancement in a range of software engineering tasks associated with source code such as code review and code generation. In this paper, we undertake a comprehensive study by instructing ChatGPT for four prevalent vulnerability tasks: function and line-level vulnerability prediction, vulnerability classification, severity estimation, and vulnerability repair. We compare ChatGPT with state-of-the-art language models designed for software vulnerability purposes. Through an empirical assessment employing extensive real-world datasets featuring over 190,000 C/C++ functions, we found that ChatGPT achieves limited performance, trailing behind other language models in vulnerability contexts by a significant margin. The experimental outcomes highlight the challenging nature of vulnerability prediction tasks, requiring domain-specific expertise. Despite ChatGPT's substantial model scale, exceeding that of source code-pre-trained language models (e.g., CodeBERT) by a factor of 14,000, the process of fine-tuning remains imperative for ChatGPT to generalize for vulnerability prediction tasks. We publish the studied dataset, experimental prompts for ChatGPT, and experimental results at https://github.com/awsm-research/ChatGPT4Vul.
summary: Evaluates ChatGPT (GPT-3.5/GPT-4) on vulnerability detection, classification, severity estimation, and repair using 190,000+ C/C++ functions, finding it trails fine-tuned CodeBERT.
summary_zh: 评估ChatGPT在漏洞检测、分类、严重性评估和修复上的表现，使用19万+C/C++函数，落后于微调的CodeBERT。
abstract_zh: ChatGPT在代码相关任务中表现出色。本文评估其在四个漏洞任务上的表现，使用19万+C/C++函数。发现其明显落后于微调模型，强调微调的重要性。

title: When Fuzzing Meets LLMs: Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Yu Jiang, Jie Liang, Fuchen Ma, Yuanliang Chen, Chijin Zhou, Yuheng Shen, Zhiyong Wu, Jingzhou Fu, Mingzhe Wang, ShanShan Li, Quan Zhang
abstract: Fuzzing, a widely-used technique for bug detection, has seen advancements through Large Language Models (LLMs). Despite their potential, LLMs face specific challenges in fuzzing. In this paper, we identified five major challenges of LLM-assisted fuzzing. To support our findings, we revisited the most recent papers from top-tier conferences, confirming that these challenges are widespread. As a remedy, we propose some actionable recommendations to help improve applying LLM in Fuzzing and conduct preliminary evaluations on DBMS fuzzing. The results demonstrate that our recommendations effectively address the identified challenges.
summary: Identifies five major challenges of LLM-assisted fuzzing from top-tier conference papers and proposes actionable recommendations validated on DBMS fuzzing.

title: How far have we gone in vulnerability detection using large language models
authors: Zeyu Gao, Hao Wang, Yuchen Zhou, Wenyu Zhu, Chao Zhang
abstract: As software becomes increasingly complex and prone to vulnerabilities, automated vulnerability detection is critically important, yet challenging. Given the significant successes of Large Language Models (LLMs) in various tasks, there is growing anticipation of their efficacy in vulnerability detection. However, a quantitative understanding of their potential in vulnerability detection is still missing. To bridge this gap, we introduce a comprehensive vulnerability benchmark VulBench. This benchmark aggregates high-quality data from a wide range of CTF (Capture-the-Flag) challenges and real-world applications, with annotations for each vulnerable function detailing the vulnerability type and its root cause. Through our experiments encompassing 16 LLMs and 6 state-of-the-art (SOTA) deep learning-based models and static analyzers, we find that several LLMs outperform traditional deep learning approaches in vulnerability detection, revealing an untapped potential in LLMs. This work contributes to the understanding and utilization of LLMs for enhanced software security.
summary: Introduces VulBench benchmark aggregating CTF and real-world data, evaluating 16 LLMs and 6 SOTA deep learning models/static analyzers, finding LLMs outperform traditional DL.
summary_zh: 引入VulBench基准，评估16个LLM和6个SOTA深度学习模型/静态分析器，发现LLM优于传统DL方法。

title: CVE-LLM: Automatic vulnerability evaluation in medical device industry using large language models
authors: Rikhiya Ghosh, Oladimeji Farri, Hans-Martin von Stockhausen, Martin Schmitt, George Marica Vasile
abstract: The healthcare industry is currently experiencing an unprecedented  wave of cybersecurity attacks, impacting millions of individuals. With the discovery of thousands of vulnerabilities each month,  there is a pressing need to drive the automation of vulnerability  assessment processes for medical devices, facilitating rapid mitigation efforts. Generative AI systems have revolutionized various  industries, offering unparalleled opportunities for automation and  increased efficiency. This paper presents a solution leveraging  Large Language Models (LLMs) to learn from historical evaluations  of vulnerabilities for the automatic assessment of vulnerabilities in  the medical devices industry. This approach is applied within the  portfolio of a single manufacturer, taking into account device characteristics, including existing security posture and controls. The  primary contributions of this paper are threefold. Firstly, it provides  a detailed examination of the best practices for training a vulnerability Language Model (LM) in an industrial context. Secondly, it  presents a comprehensive comparison and insightful analysis of the  effectiveness of Language Models in vulnerability assessment. Finally, it proposes a new human-in-the-loop framework to expedite  vulnerability evaluation processes.
summary: Presents CVE-LLM leveraging LLMs trained on historical evaluations for automatic vulnerability assessment in medical devices, proposing a human-in-the-loop framework.

title: Scope: Evaluating llms for software vulnerability detection
authors: Gon{\c{c
abstract: In recent years, code security has become increasingly important, especially with the rise of interconnected technologies. Detecting vulnerabilities early in the software development process has demonstrated numerous benefits. Consequently, the scientific community started using machine learning for automated detection of source code vulnerabilities. This work explores and refines the CVEFixes dataset, which is commonly used to train models for code-related tasks, specifically the C/C++ subset. To this purpose, the Source Code Processing Engine (SCoPE), a framework composed of strategized techniques that can be used to reduce the size and normalize C/C++ functions is presented. The output generated by SCoPE was used to create a new version of CVEFixes. This refined dataset was then employed in a feature representation analysis to assess the effectiveness of the tool's code processing techniques, consisting of fine-tuning three pre-trained LLMs for software vulnerability detection. The results show that SCoPE successfully helped to identify 905 duplicates within the evaluated subset. The LLM results corroborate with the literature regarding their suitability for software vulnerability detection, with the best model achieving 53% F1-score.
summary: Proposes SCoPE framework for C/C++ code normalization, refines CVEFixes dataset by identifying 905 duplicates, and fine-tunes three LLMs achieving best 53% F1-score.

title: Outside the comfort zone: Analysing llm capabilities in software vulnerability detection
authors: Yuejun Guo, Constantinos Patsakis, Qiang Hu, Qiang Tang, Fran Casino
venue: European symposium on research in computer security
abstract: The significant increase in software production driven by automation and faster development lifecycles has resulted in a corresponding surge in software vulnerabilities. In parallel, the evolving landscape of software vulnerability detection, highlighting the shift from traditional methods to machine learning and large language models (LLMs), provides massive opportunities at the cost of resource-demanding computations. This paper thoroughly analyses LLMs’ capabilities in detecting vulnerabilities within source code by testing models beyond their usual applications to study their potential in cybersecurity tasks. We evaluate the performance of six open-source models that are specifically trained for vulnerability detection against six general-purpose LLMs, three of which were further fine-tuned on a dataset that we compiled. Our dataset, alongside five state-of-the-art benchmark datasets, were used to create a pipeline to leverage a binary classification task, namely classifying code into vulnerable and non-vulnerable. The findings highlight significant variations in classification accuracy across benchmarks, revealing the critical influence of fine-tuning in enhancing the detection capabilities of small LLMs over their larger counterparts, yet only in the specific scenarios in which they were trained. Further experiments and analysis also underscore the issues with current benchmark datasets, particularly around mislabeling and their impact on model training and performance, which raises concerns about the current state of practice. We also discuss the road ahead in the field suggesting strategies for improved model training and dataset curation.
summary: Evaluates 6 vulnerability-specific and 6 general-purpose LLMs (3 fine-tuned) on 5 benchmark datasets for binary vulnerability classification, highlighting fine-tuning benefits for small LLMs.
summary_zh: 在5个基准数据集上评估6个漏洞专用和6个通用LLM，突显微调对小型LLM的优势。

title: Advanced Vulnerability Detection Using Llm with Transparency Obligation Practice Towards Trustworthy Ai
authors: Haurogn{e
abstract: Vulnerabilities in the source code are the primary causes of potential threats in software systems. There are a large number of vulnerabilities published each day, and effective vulnerability detection is critical to identifying and mitigating these vulnerabilities. AI has emerged as a promising solution to enhance vulnerability detection. However, AI-based methods often face challenges with large datasets and understanding the problem's specific context. In this context, the industry and research communities now widely consider the Large Language Model (LLM) to tackle more complex tasks and large datasets. However, LLM also exhibits limitations in terms of explaining the model outcome for a specific context due to its black box nature. To address this challenge, this research introduces an advanced vulnerability detection approach using LLMs, specifically the BERT model. Additionally, the approach introduces key dimensions of transparency obligations, model outcome and explainability, which ensure the transparency of decision-making and trustworthy AI practice. We address the black-box nature of LLMs by employing XAI techniques, particularly SHAP to provide clear explanations for the model’s decisions. We propose an architecture that combines the BERT model with transparency obligation practices, which ensures the assurance of transparency dimensions throughout the entire LLM life cycle. An experiment is performed with a source code dataset to demonstrate the applicability of the proposed approach. The result shows an average accuracy of 69% for the chosen model in vulnerability detection. Moreover, the transparency obligation practice is effectively implemented through the entire lifecycle of the LLM model towards trustworthy AI-enabled system development.
summary: Proposes BERT-based vulnerability detection with XAI (SHAP) transparency obligations for trustworthy AI, achieving average 69% accuracy on source code dataset.
summary_zh: 提出基于BERT的漏洞检测，结合SHAP透明度义务实现可信AI，准确率达69%。

Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Average accuracy of 69% for vulnerability detection。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Malicious Code Detection Using LLM
authors: Al Amin Hossain, Mithun Kumar PK, Junjie Zhang, Fathi Amsaad
venue: NAECON 2024-IEEE National Aerospace and Electronics Conference
abstract:
In the context of the rising interest in code language models (code LMs) and vulnerability detection, we study the effectiveness of code LMs for detecting vulnerabilities. Our analysis reveals significant shortcomings in existing vulnerability datasets, including poor data quality, low label accuracy, and high duplication rates, leading to unreliable model performance in realistic vulnerability detection scenarios. Additionally, the evaluation methods used with these datasets are not representative of real-world vulnerability detection.
To address these challenges, we introduce PrimeVul, a new dataset for training and evaluating code LMs for vulnerability detection. PrimeVul incorporates a novel set of data labeling techniques that achieve comparable label accuracy to human-verified benchmarks while significantly expanding the dataset. It also implements a rigorous data de-duplication and chronological data splitting strategy to mitigate data leakage issues, alongside introducing more realistic evaluation metrics and settings. This comprehensive approach aims to provide a more accurate assessment of code LMs' performance in real-world conditions.
Evaluating code LMs on PrimeVul reveals that existing benchmarks significantly overestimate the performance of these models. For instance, a state-of-the-art 7B model scored 68.26% F1 on BigVul but only 3.09% F1 on PrimeVul. Attempts to improve performance through advanced training techniques and larger models like GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 were unsuccessful, with results akin to random guessing in the most stringent settings. These findings underscore the considerable gap between current capabilities and the practical requirements for deploying code LMs in security roles, highlighting the need for more innovative research in this domain.
summary: Introduces PrimeVul dataset for code LM vulnerability detection, showing SOTA 7B model drops from 68.26% F1 on BigVul to 3.09% on PrimeVul; GPT-3.5/GPT-4 near random.
summary_zh: 引入PrimeVul数据集，SOTA 7B模型从BigVul 68.26% F1降至PrimeVul 3.09%；GPT-3.5/GPT-4接近随机。
abstract_zh: 研究代码语言模型在漏洞检测中的有效性。引入PrimeVul数据集，SOTA 7B模型在BigVul上68.26% F1但PrimeVul上仅3.09%。GPT-3.5/GPT-4接近随机。

Introduces PrimeVul with novel labeling techniques.
Implements rigorous deduplication and chronological splitting.
SOTA 7B model drops from 68.26% F1 on BigVul to 3.09% on PrimeVul.
GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 produce results akin to random guessing.

引入PrimeVul数据集。
实施严格去重和按时间分割。
SOTA 7B模型从68.26% F1降至3.09%。
GPT-3.5/GPT-4接近随机。

未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Large language model-powered smart contract vulnerability detection: New perspectives
authors: Hu, Sihao, Huang, Tiansheng, {\.I
venue: 2023 5th IEEE International Conference on Trust, Privacy and Security in Intelligent Systems and Applications (TPS-ISA)
abstract: This paper provides a systematic analysis of the opportunities, challenges, and potential solutions of harnessing Large Language Models (LLMs) such as GPT-4 to dig out vulnerabilities within smart contracts based on our ongoing research. For the task of smart contract vulnerability detection, achieving practical usability hinges on identifying as many true vulnerabilities as possible while minimizing the number of false positives. Nonetheless, our empirical study reveals contradictory yet interesting findings: generating more answers with higher randomness largely boosts the likelihood of producing a correct answer but inevitably leads to a higher number of false positives. To mitigate this tension, we propose an adversarial framework dubbed GPTL ENS that breaks the conventional one-stage detection into two synergistic stages - generation and discrimination, for progressive detection and refinement, wherein the LLM plays dual roles, i.e., AUDITOR and CRITIC, respectively. The goal of AUDITOR is to yield a broad spectrum of vulnerabilities with the hope of encompassing the correct answer, whereas the goal of CRITIC that evaluates the validity of identified vulnerabilities is to minimize the number of false positives. Experimental results and illustrative examples demonstrate that AUDITOR and CRITIC work together harmoniously to yield pronounced improvements over the conventional one-stage detection. GPTL ENS is intuitive, strategic, and entirely LLM-driven without relying on specialist expertise in smart contracts, showcasing its methodical generality and potential to detect a broad spectrum of vulnerabilities. Our code is available at: https://github.com/git-disl/GPTLens.
summary: Proposes GPTLens, an adversarial two-stage LLM framework (Auditor+Critic) for smart contract vulnerability detection, improving over one-stage detection.
summary_zh: 提出GPTLens，一种对抗性两阶段LLM框架（审计者+评论者），用于智能合约漏洞检测，优于单阶段检测方法。
contributions: Proposes GPTLens, a two-stage adversarial framework using LLM as both Auditor and Critic for smart contract vulnerability detection.
Reveals that higher randomness in LLM answers increases true positive rates but also false positives.
Introduces a generation-discrimination pipeline that progressively detects and refines vulnerability findings.
Demonstrates that GPTLens is entirely LLM-driven without requiring specialist smart contract expertise.
Releases open-source code at https://github.com/git-disl/GPTLens.
contributions_zh: 提出GPTLens，一种使用LLM同时作为审计者和评论者的两阶段对抗框架，用于智能合约漏洞检测。
揭示LLM回答中更高的随机性增加了真阳性率但也增加了误报率。
引入生成-判别流水线，逐步检测和优化漏洞发现结果。
证明GPTLens完全由LLM驱动，无需智能合约专业知识。
在https://github.com/git-disl/GPTLens开源代码。
abstract_zh: 本文系统分析了利用GPT-4等大语言模型（LLM）挖掘智能合约漏洞的机遇、挑战和潜在解决方案。对于智能合约漏洞检测任务，实现实际可用性取决于尽可能多地识别真实漏洞，同时最小化误报数量。然而，我们的实证研究揭示了矛盾但有趣的发现：以更高随机性生成更多答案大大提高了产生正确答案的可能性，但不可避免地导致更多误报。为了缓解这种矛盾，我们提出了一个名为GPTLens的对抗框架，将传统的单阶段检测分解为两个协同阶段——生成和判别，进行渐进式检测和优化，其中LLM扮演双重角色，即审计者（AUDITOR）和评论者（CRITIC）。审计者的目标是产生广泛的漏洞范围，以期涵盖正确答案，而评论者评估已识别漏洞有效性的目标是最小化误报数量。实验结果和示例表明，审计者和评论者协同工作，相比传统单阶段检测产生了显著改进。GPTLens直观、策略性强，完全由LLM驱动，不依赖智能合约专业知识，展示了其方法的通用性和检测广泛漏洞的潜力。代码可在https://github.com/git-disl/GPTLens获取。

experiment.key_results: GPTLens with dual Auditor-Critic roles yields pronounced improvements over conventional one-stage detection.

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
Can LLMs effectively identify logic vulnerabilities that traditional tools miss in smart contracts?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: GPTLens with dual Auditor-Critic roles yields pronounced improvements over conventional one-stage detection.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：GPTLens with dual Auditor-Critic roles yields pronounced improvements over conventional one-stage detection。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: LSAST--Enhancing Cybersecurity through LLM-supported Static Application Security Testing
authors: Mete Keltek, Rong Hu, Mohammadreza Fani Sani, Ziyue Li
abstract: The current cybersecurity landscape is increasingly complex, with traditional Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tools struggling to capture complex and emerging vulnerabilities due to their reliance on rule-based matching. Meanwhile, Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated powerful code analysis capabilities, but their static training data and privacy risks limit their effectiveness. To overcome the limitations of both approaches, we propose LSAST, a novel approach that integrates LLMs with SAST scanners to enhance vulnerability detection. LSAST leverages a locally hostable LLM, combined with a state-of-the-art knowledge retrieval system, to provide up-to-date vulnerability insights without compromising data privacy. We set a new benchmark for static vulnerability analysis, offering a robust, privacy-conscious solution that bridges the gap between traditional scanners and advanced AI-driven analysis. Our evaluation demonstrates that incorporating SAST results into LLM analysis significantly improves detection accuracy, identifying vulnerabilities missed by conventional methods.
summary: Proposes LSAST, integrating a locally-hosted LLM with SAST scanners and knowledge retrieval to improve vulnerability detection while preserving data privacy.
summary_zh: 提出LSAST，将本地托管的LLM与SAST扫描器和知识检索系统集成，在保护数据隐私的同时提高漏洞检测能力。
contributions: Proposes LSAST, a novel approach integrating LLMs with SAST scanners for enhanced vulnerability detection.
Uses a locally hostable LLM combined with a state-of-the-art knowledge retrieval system to ensure data privacy.
Sets a new benchmark for static vulnerability analysis with a privacy-conscious solution.
Demonstrates that incorporating SAST results into LLM analysis significantly improves detection accuracy.
contributions_zh: 提出LSAST，一种将LLM与SAST扫描器集成的新方法，用于增强漏洞检测。
使用本地可托管的LLM结合先进的知识检索系统，确保数据隐私。
为静态漏洞分析设立了新的基准，提供了隐私感知的解决方案。
证明将SAST结果纳入LLM分析可显著提高检测准确性。
abstract_zh: 当前网络安全形势日益复杂，传统的静态应用安全测试（SAST）工具由于依赖基于规则的匹配，在捕获复杂和新兴漏洞方面面临困难。同时，大语言模型（LLM）展示了强大的代码分析能力，但其静态训练数据和隐私风险限制了其有效性。为了克服这两种方法的局限性，我们提出了LSAST，一种将LLM与SAST扫描器集成以增强漏洞检测的新方法。LSAST利用本地可托管的LLM，结合先进的知识检索系统，在不损害数据隐私的情况下提供最新的漏洞洞察。我们为静态漏洞分析设立了新的基准，提供了一个稳健的、隐私感知的解决方案，弥合了传统扫描器与先进AI驱动分析之间的差距。我们的评估表明，将SAST结果纳入LLM分析可显著提高检测准确性，识别出传统方法遗漏的漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Incorporating SAST results into LLM analysis significantly improves detection accuracy, identifying vulnerabilities missed by conventional methods.

当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Incorporating SAST results into LLM analysis significantly improves detection accuracy, identifying vulnerabilities missed by conventional methods.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.

未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: Understanding the effectiveness of large language models in detecting security vulnerabilities
authors: Avishree Khare, Saikat Dutta, Ziyang Li, Alaia Solko-Breslin, Rajeev Alur, Mayur Naik
abstract:
While automated vulnerability detection techniques have made promising progress in detecting security vulnerabilities, their scalability and applicability remain challenging. The remarkable performance of Large Language Models (LLMs), such as GPT-4 and CodeLlama, on code-related tasks has prompted recent works to explore if LLMs can be used to detect vulnerabilities. In this paper, we perform a more comprehensive study by concurrently examining a higher number of datasets, languages and LLMs, and qualitatively evaluating performance across prompts and vulnerability classes while addressing the shortcomings of existing tools. Concretely, we evaluate the effectiveness of 16 pre-trained LLMs on 5,000 code samples from five diverse security datasets. These balanced datasets encompass both synthetic and real-world projects in Java and C/C++ and cover 25 distinct vulnerability classes.
Overall, LLMs across all scales and families show modest effectiveness in detecting vulnerabilities, obtaining an average accuracy of 62.8% and F1 score of 0.71 across datasets. They are significantly better at detecting vulnerabilities only requiring intra-procedural analysis, such as OS Command Injection and NULL Pointer Dereference. Moreover, they report higher accuracies on these vulnerabilities than popular static analysis tools, such as CodeQL.
We find that advanced prompting strategies that involve step-by-step analysis significantly improve performance of LLMs on real-world datasets in terms of F1 score (by upto 0.18 on average). Interestingly, we observe that LLMs show promising abilities at performing parts of the analysis correctly, such as identifying vulnerability-related specifications and leveraging natural language information to understand code behavior (e.g., to check if code is sanitized). We expect our insights to guide future work on LLM-augmented vulnerability detection systems.
summary: Evaluates 16 pre-trained LLMs on 5,000 code samples across 5 datasets in Java and C/C++, finding 62.8% average accuracy with step-by-step prompting improving F1 by up to 0.18.
summary_zh: 在Java和C/C++的5个数据集上评估16个预训练LLM对5000个代码样本的表现，平均准确率62.8%，逐步提示策略将F1提升最高0.18。
contributions: Evaluates 16 pre-trained LLMs on 5,000 code samples from five diverse security datasets covering Java and C/C++.
Finds LLMs achieve an average accuracy of 62.8% and F1 score of 0.71 across datasets, with better performance on intra-procedural vulnerabilities.
Shows LLMs report higher accuracies than CodeQL on vulnerabilities like OS Command Injection and NULL Pointer Dereference.
Demonstrates that step-by-step prompting strategies improve F1 score by up to 0.18 on real-world datasets.
Identifies LLM strengths in vulnerability-related specification identification and natural language-based code behavior understanding.
contributions_zh: 在覆盖Java和C/C++的五个多样化安全数据集上评估了16个预训练LLM对5000个代码样本的表现。
发现LLM在各数据集上平均准确率为62.8%，F1分数为0.71，在过程内漏洞上表现更好。
表明LLM在OS命令注入和空指针解引用等漏洞上的准确率高于CodeQL。
证明逐步提示策略在真实世界数据集上将F1分数提高了最多0.18。
识别了LLM在漏洞相关规范识别和基于自然语言的代码行为理解方面的优势。
abstract_zh: 虽然自动化漏洞检测技术在检测安全漏洞方面取得了可喜的进展，但其可扩展性和适用性仍然面临挑战。GPT-4和CodeLlama等大语言模型（LLM）在代码相关任务上的卓越表现促使近期研究探索LLM能否用于检测漏洞。本文通过同时检验更多的数据集、语言和LLM进行了更全面的研究，并在定性评估不同提示和漏洞类别的性能的同时解决了现有工具的不足。具体而言，我们评估了16个预训练LLM在五个多样化安全数据集的5000个代码样本上的有效性。这些平衡数据集涵盖了Java和C/C++的合成和真实世界项目，覆盖25种不同的漏洞类别。总体而言，所有规模和系列的LLM在检测漏洞方面表现一般，在各数据集上获得了62.8%的平均准确率和0.71的F1分数。它们在只需要过程内分析的漏洞检测上明显更好，如OS命令注入和空指针解引用。此外，它们在这些漏洞上的准确率高于流行的静态分析工具如CodeQL。我们发现，涉及逐步分析的高级提示策略在真实世界数据集上显著提高了LLM的F1分数（平均提高了多达0.18）。有趣的是，我们观察到LLM在正确执行部分分析方面展示了有前途的能力，如识别漏洞相关规范和利用自然语言信息理解代码行为。我们期望我们的洞察能指导未来LLM增强漏洞检测系统的研究工作。

conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Average accuracy of 62.8% and F1 score of 0.71; step-by-step prompting improves F1 by up to 0.18.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
