title: WhiteFox: White-Box Compiler Fuzzing Empowered by Large Language Models
authors: Chenyuan Yang, Yinlin Deng, Runyu Lu, Jiayi Yao, Jiawei Liu, Reyhaneh Jabbarvand, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Compiler correctness is crucial, as miscompilation can falsify program execution. Fuzzing has been used extensively to uncover compiler defects. However, existing methods are limited by the inability to target specific optimization passes. We propose WhiteFox, the first white-box compiler fuzzer using LLMs. WhiteFox analyzes compiler source code to understand optimization triggers and generates test programs that specifically exercise those code paths.
summary: Proposes WhiteFox, white-box compiler fuzzer using LLMs to analyze optimization source code and generate targeted test programs, finding 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM.
summary_zh: 提出WhiteFox，使用LLM分析优化源代码并生成针对性测试程序的白盒编译器模糊测试器，在GCC和LLVM中发现101个漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM, 96 confirmed
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs confirmed and fixed in GCC and LLVM trunk

contributions: Proposes WhiteFox, a novel approach for white-box compiler fuzzer using LLMs to analyze optimization source code and generate targeted test programs, finding 10.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 3 baseline approaches including CSmith, YARPGen, Fuzz4All.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 101 bugs in GCC and LLVM, 96 confirmed.
Addresses miscompilation, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: KernelGPT: Enhanced Kernel Fuzzing via Large Language Models
authors: Chenyuan Yang, Zijie Zou, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Kernel fuzzing is essential for detecting vulnerabilities in operating systems. Syzkaller, the state-of-the-art kernel fuzzer, requires manually written specifications (syzlang) to describe syscall interfaces. This process is tedious and error-prone, leaving many interfaces under-tested. We propose KernelGPT, a novel approach leveraging LLMs to automatically generate Syzkaller specifications from kernel source code and documentation.
summary: Proposes KernelGPT using LLMs to automatically generate Syzkaller specifications for kernel fuzzing, improving syscall coverage and finding new kernel bugs.
summary_zh: 提出KernelGPT，利用LLM自动生成Syzkaller规范用于内核模糊测试，提升系统调用覆盖率并发现新的内核漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Generated specs for 113 new drivers, found 11 new kernel bugs
experiment.real_world_impact: New bugs found in Linux kernel drivers

contributions: Proposes KernelGPT, a novel approach for using LLMs to automatically generate Syzkaller specifications for kernel fuzzing, improving syscall coverage and finding.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 3 baseline approaches including Syzkaller (manual specs), SyzDescribe, KSG.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generated specs for 113 new drivers, found 11 new kernel bugs.
Addresses kernel-bug, use-after-free, null-pointer vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: FuzzGPT: Synthesizing Fuzzing Test Cases via Large Language Models
authors: Yinlin Deng, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Chenyuan Yang, Shujing Zhang, Shengqi Yang, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Fuzzing is essential for software testing, but generating effective test inputs remains challenging. We propose FuzzGPT, which leverages LLMs to synthesize high-quality fuzzing test cases by learning from historical bug-triggering inputs. FuzzGPT uses in-context learning with carefully curated examples of past vulnerability-triggering inputs to guide the LLM in generating similar edge-case inputs.
summary: Proposes FuzzGPT using LLMs with in-context learning from historical bug-triggering inputs to synthesize effective fuzzing test cases for JavaScript engines and compilers.
summary_zh: 提出FuzzGPT，利用LLM通过上下文学习历史触发漏洞的输入来合成有效的模糊测试用例，用于JavaScript引擎和编译器。

experiment.key_results: Found 76 bugs across JS engines and compilers, 49 previously unknown
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs found in V8, SpiderMonkey, JavaScriptCore

Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 76 bugs across JS engines and compilers, 49 previously unknown.
Addresses crash, assertion-failure vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: AI-Powered Fuzzing: Breaking the Bug Hunting Barrier
authors: Jonathan Metzman, Laszlo Szekeres, Laurent Simon, Read Sprabery, Abhishek Arya
abstract: We present OSS-Fuzz-Gen, an LLM-based framework for automatically generating fuzz targets for open-source software projects. By leveraging LLMs' understanding of code and API semantics, OSS-Fuzz-Gen analyzes project source code and generates effective fuzz harnesses that can be integrated into the OSS-Fuzz continuous fuzzing pipeline.
summary: Google's OSS-Fuzz-Gen uses LLMs to automatically generate fuzz targets for open-source projects, significantly expanding fuzzing coverage in OSS-Fuzz.
summary_zh: Google的OSS-Fuzz-Gen使用LLM自动为开源项目生成模糊测试目标，显著扩大OSS-Fuzz的模糊测试覆盖范围。

experiment.key_results: Generated harnesses for 160+ projects, increased code coverage by 30%
experiment.real_world_impact: Deployed in Google OSS-Fuzz pipeline

title: PromptFuzz: Harnessing Fuzzing Techniques for Robust Testing of Prompt Injection in LLMs
authors: Jiahao Lyu, Yiqi Meng, Zhiyi Li
abstract: Large Language Models are susceptible to prompt injection attacks. We propose PromptFuzz, a fuzzing-based framework that systematically tests LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities by generating diverse adversarial prompts using mutation-based and generation-based fuzzing techniques.
summary: Proposes PromptFuzz, applying fuzzing techniques to systematically test LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities through adversarial prompt generation.
summary_zh: 提出PromptFuzz，应用模糊测试技术通过对抗性提示生成系统地测试LLM的提示注入漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Discovered 200+ prompt injection variants across 3 major LLMs
experiment.real_world_impact: Identified bypasses in production LLM systems

contributions: Proposes PromptFuzz, a novel approach for applying fuzzing techniques to systematically test LLMs for prompt injection vulnerabilities through adversarial prompt.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 1 baseline approaches including Manual prompt testing.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Discovered 200+ prompt injection variants across 3 major LLMs.
Addresses prompt-injection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: InputBlaster: Generating Diverse Crash-Inducing Inputs for Android Apps via LLM
authors: Zhe Liu, Chunyang Chen, Junjie Chen, Mengzhuo Xia, Boyu Wang
abstract: Mobile app testing requires generating diverse inputs that can trigger crashes. We present InputBlaster, a framework that leverages LLMs to understand UI context and generate semantically valid yet boundary-testing inputs for Android apps. By analyzing app UI elements and their expected input types, InputBlaster generates targeted crash-inducing inputs.
summary: Proposes InputBlaster using LLMs to generate diverse crash-inducing inputs for Android apps by understanding UI context and semantics.
summary_zh: 提出InputBlaster，利用LLM理解UI上下文和语义为Android应用生成多样化的崩溃触发输入。

experiment.key_results: Found 118 unique crashes, 73% more than baselines

Leverages GPT-3.5, GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 36 Android apps benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 118 unique crashes, 73% more than baselines.
Addresses crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: LlamaFuzz: Large Language Model Enhanced Greybox Fuzzing
authors: Hongwei Zhang, Zhuo Zhao, Birui Zhang, Yifeng Chen
abstract: Greybox fuzzing is one of the most effective vulnerability detection techniques. However, mutating structured inputs while maintaining their validity remains challenging. We present LlamaFuzz, which integrates fine-tuned LLMs into greybox fuzzing to generate structure-aware mutations. By fine-tuning Llama on a corpus of valid inputs, LlamaFuzz learns input grammar and produces mutations that maintain structural validity while exploring edge cases.
summary: Proposes LlamaFuzz integrating fine-tuned Llama models into greybox fuzzing for structure-aware mutations, improving coverage on structured input formats.

title: ChatAFL: A Large Language Model Guided Protocol Fuzzer
authors: Ruijie Meng, Martin Mirber, Marcel Bockmann, Frank Bsser, Thorsten Holz
abstract: Protocol implementations are essential components of network infrastructure, making their security critical. We present ChatAFL, a protocol fuzzer guided by LLMs. ChatAFL leverages the LLM's understanding of protocol specifications to generate semantically valid protocol messages, enriching the fuzzer's message pool with structure-aware inputs.
summary: Proposes ChatAFL, an LLM-guided protocol fuzzer that generates semantically valid protocol messages for testing network protocol implementations.
summary_zh: 提出ChatAFL，一种LLM引导的协议模糊测试器，为测试网络协议实现生成语义有效的协议消息。

title: Harnessing Large Language Models for Seed Generation in Greybox Fuzzing
authors: Wenxuan Li, Kexin Pei, Shweta Ma
abstract: The effectiveness of greybox fuzzing depends significantly on the quality of initial seed inputs. We propose using LLMs to generate high-quality seed inputs by analyzing target program source code and documentation. Our approach prompts LLMs to understand input format requirements and generate diverse, valid initial seeds.
summary: Uses LLMs to generate high-quality initial seed inputs for greybox fuzzing by analyzing target program code and documentation.
summary_zh: 利用LLM通过分析目标程序代码和文档为灰盒模糊测试生成高质量初始种子输入。

experiment.key_results: 22% faster bug finding, 18% higher edge coverage with LLM seeds

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to generate high-quality initial seed inputs for greybox fuzzing by analyzing target program code and documentation.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on Magma, FuzzBench benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 22% faster bug finding, 18% higher edge coverage with LLM seeds.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

title: Augmenting Greybox Fuzzing with Generative AI
authors: Jie Hu, Qian Ruan, Hao Pei, Cen Li
abstract: Greybox fuzzers like AFL struggle with programs that process structured inputs due to format constraints. We propose augmenting greybox fuzzing with generative AI models that learn input structure from existing test corpora. Our approach uses a fine-tuned language model to generate format-compliant mutations that bypass input validation while exploring deep program paths.
summary: Augments greybox fuzzing with generative AI to produce format-compliant mutations, improving deep path exploration for structured input programs.
summary_zh: 用生成式AI增强灰盒模糊测试，产生格式合规的变异，改善结构化输入程序的深层路径探索。

experiment.key_results: 31% more bugs found than AFL++ on structured inputs

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for augments greybox fuzzing with generative AI to produce format-compliant mutations, improving deep path exploration for s.
Leverages CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 31% more bugs found than AFL++ on structured inputs.

title: Automatically Generating Fuzz Drivers via Large Language Models
authors: Cen Wang, Mingzhe Li, Jie Zhang, Yang Liu
abstract: Writing fuzz drivers (harnesses) for library APIs is a labor-intensive but essential task for effective fuzzing. We propose an LLM-based approach to automatically generate fuzz drivers by analyzing library API documentation, header files, and usage examples. Our method uses multi-turn LLM interaction with feedback-driven refinement.
summary: Automatically generates fuzz drivers for library APIs using LLMs with multi-turn interaction and feedback-driven refinement.
summary_zh: 使用LLM通过多轮交互和反馈驱动的细化自动为库API生成模糊测试驱动程序。

experiment.key_results: 73% of generated harnesses compile and achieve >60% API coverage
experiment.real_world_impact: Found 15 previously unknown bugs in popular libraries

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for automatically generates fuzz drivers for library APIs using LLMs with multi-turn interaction and feedback-driven refinem.
Leverages GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 10 popular C libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 73% of generated harnesses compile and achieve >60% API coverage.
Addresses memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: FuzzCraft: LLM-Assisted Fuzzing Harness Generation for C/C++ Libraries
authors: Xiaofei Xie, Ting Liu, Shang Li, Jun Chen
abstract: Fuzzing is highly effective for finding vulnerabilities but requires well-crafted harnesses to exercise library APIs effectively. We present FuzzCraft, an LLM-assisted system that automatically generates compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries by analyzing API signatures, documentation, and existing test cases.
summary: Proposes FuzzCraft for LLM-assisted generation of compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries using API analysis.
summary_zh: 提出FuzzCraft，通过API分析实现LLM辅助的C/C++库编译就绪模糊测试桩生成。

experiment.key_results: 82% compilation rate, found 23 new bugs across 15 libraries
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs found in libxml2, zlib, openssl

contributions: Proposes FuzzCraft, a novel approach for for LLM-assisted generation of compilation-ready fuzzing harnesses for C/C++ libraries using API analysis.

Evaluates on 15 popular C/C++ libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 82% compilation rate, found 23 new bugs across 15 libraries.
Addresses memory-safety, crash, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Muzeel: Generating Structured Test Programs for Fuzzing JavaScript Engines
authors: Haichuan Chen, Shengqi Yang, Yinlin Deng, Lingming Zhang
abstract: JavaScript engine bugs can lead to severe security vulnerabilities. We present Muzeel, which uses LLMs to generate structured test programs targeting JavaScript engine internals. Muzeel combines LLM-based program generation with coverage-guided feedback to efficiently explore JS engine code paths.
summary: Proposes Muzeel using LLMs to generate structured JavaScript test programs with coverage-guided feedback for JS engine fuzzing.
summary_zh: 提出Muzeel，使用LLM生成结构化JavaScript测试程序，结合覆盖引导反馈进行JS引擎模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: Found 42 bugs in V8, SpiderMonkey, JSC, 18 CVEs assigned
experiment.real_world_impact: 18 CVEs in production JavaScript engines

contributions: Proposes Muzeel, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate structured JavaScript test programs with coverage-guided feedback for JS engine fuzzing.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 4 baseline approaches including Montage, DIE, Fuzzilli.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 42 bugs in V8, SpiderMonkey, JSC, 18 CVEs assigned.
Addresses crash, type-confusion, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: API-Level Fuzz Driver Generation with LLM-Based Program Synthesis
authors: Zhengjie Zhao, Yue Wang, Mark Chen
abstract: Generating effective API-level fuzz drivers requires understanding API semantics, parameter constraints, and calling conventions. We propose an LLM-based program synthesis approach that generates fuzz drivers by learning from API documentation, existing test suites, and usage patterns in client code.
summary: Generates API-level fuzz drivers through LLM-based program synthesis using documentation, test suites, and client code patterns.
summary_zh: 通过基于LLM的程序合成利用文档、测试套件和客户端代码模式生成API级模糊测试驱动程序。

Evaluates on 20 open-source libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 68% of drivers compile correctly, 45% achieve >70% code coverage.

title: SmartFuzz: LLM-Enhanced Fuzzing for Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection
authors: Lei Wu, Peng Zhang, Jianbo Chen, Xiapu Luo
abstract: Smart contract vulnerabilities have caused billions in financial losses. We present SmartFuzz, which leverages LLMs to generate targeted fuzzing inputs for Solidity smart contracts. SmartFuzz uses the LLM's understanding of Solidity semantics to craft transaction sequences that trigger specific vulnerability patterns.
summary: Proposes SmartFuzz using LLMs to generate targeted transaction sequences for fuzzing Solidity smart contracts, detecting reentrancy and other vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 提出SmartFuzz，利用LLM生成针对性交易序列来模糊测试Solidity智能合约，检测重入和其他漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 35 zero-day vulnerabilities in DeFi protocols
experiment.real_world_impact: Responsible disclosure to 12 DeFi projects

contributions: Proposes SmartFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate targeted transaction sequences for fuzzing Solidity smart contracts, detecting reentrancy and oth.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on SmartBugs, DeFi protocols benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 35 zero-day vulnerabilities in DeFi protocols.
Addresses reentrancy, integer-overflow, access-control vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 SmartFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate targeted transaction sequences for fuzzing Solidity smart contracts, detecting reentrancy and oth.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

解决 reentrancy, integer-overflow, access-control vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

未来工作应探索LLM推理与覆盖率反馈的更紧密集成。

title: From RFCs to Fuzz Targets: Automated Protocol Fuzzing with LLMs
authors: Yuxuan Chen, Roberto Natella, Domenico Cotroneo
abstract: Network protocol implementations must conform to RFC specifications, but manual testing is insufficient to ensure correctness. We propose an approach that uses LLMs to read RFC documents and automatically generate fuzz targets that exercise protocol state machines according to the specification.
summary: Uses LLMs to read RFC documents and automatically generate protocol fuzz targets that exercise state machines according to specifications.
summary_zh: 利用LLM阅读RFC文档并自动生成按照规范执行状态机的协议模糊测试目标。

experiment.key_results: Generated harnesses achieved 85% of manual harness coverage

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to read RFC documents and automatically generate protocol fuzz targets that exercise state machines according to sp.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 6 protocol implementations benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generated harnesses achieved 85% of manual harness coverage.
Addresses crash, RFC-violation vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to read RFC documents and automatically generate protocol fuzz targets that exercise state machines according to sp.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 6 protocol implementations benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Generated harnesses achieved 85% of manual harness coverage.
解决 crash, RFC-violation vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

research_questions: Can LLMs automatically generate correct and compilable fuzz harnesses for complex APIs?
How does the code coverage achieved by LLM-generated harnesses compare to manually written ones?
What techniques are most effective for handling compilation errors in LLM-generated harnesses?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否自动生成正确且可编译的复杂API模糊测试桩？
LLM生成的桩实现的代码覆盖率与手动编写的相比如何？
处理LLM生成桩中编译错误的最有效技术是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Generated harnesses achieved 85% of manual harness coverage.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Generated harnesses achieved 85% of manual harness coverage。
研究发现为从事LLM安全工具研究的研究人员和从业者提供了实用见解。

title: AutoFuzz: Automated Fuzz Driver Generation Using LLM-Guided Analysis
authors: Seunghoon Lee, Dongkwan Kim, Sang Kil Cha
abstract: Fuzz drivers are crucial for library fuzzing, but crafting them manually is laborious. We propose AutoFuzz, which uses LLMs guided by static analysis to automatically generate effective fuzz drivers. AutoFuzz combines type-aware API analysis with LLM-based code generation and iterative compilation feedback.
summary: Proposes AutoFuzz combining static analysis with LLM-guided code generation and iterative compilation feedback for automatic fuzz driver generation.
summary_zh: 提出AutoFuzz，结合静态分析与LLM引导的代码生成和迭代编译反馈，实现自动模糊测试驱动程序生成。

experiment.key_results: 88% compilation rate, 25% more coverage than FUDGE
experiment.real_world_impact: Found 9 new bugs in libpng, libjpeg, sqlite

contributions: Proposes AutoFuzz, a novel approach for combining static analysis with LLM-guided code generation and iterative compilation feedback for automatic fuzz driver g.
Leverages GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 12 libraries from FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 88% compilation rate, 25% more coverage than FUDGE.
Addresses memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

解决 memory-safety, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Grammar-Aware Fuzzing with LLM-Generated Input Specifications
authors: Hongliang Liang, Yu Zhang, Mathias Payer
abstract: Grammar-based fuzzers require input grammars that are often manually written. We propose using LLMs to automatically infer input grammars from program source code and documentation. The inferred grammars are then used to guide structure-aware fuzzing, combining the LLM's language understanding with traditional fuzzing effectiveness.
summary: Uses LLMs to automatically infer input grammars from source code and documentation for grammar-aware fuzzing.
summary_zh: 利用LLM从源代码和文档自动推断输入语法用于语法感知模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: LLM-inferred grammars achieved 92% of manual grammar coverage

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to automatically infer input grammars from source code and documentation for grammar-aware fuzzing.
Leverages GPT-4, StarCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 10 format parsers benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLM-inferred grammars achieved 92% of manual grammar coverage.
Addresses crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4, StarCoder for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

解决 crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: RustFuzz: LLM-Powered Fuzz Target Generation for Rust Libraries
authors: Zhiyong Ren, Xiaoguang Mao, Yan Cai
abstract: Rust's memory safety guarantees at compile time make it challenging for traditional fuzzers. We present RustFuzz, which uses LLMs to generate fuzz targets specifically designed for Rust libraries, handling Rust's ownership system, lifetimes, and trait constraints. RustFuzz generates valid Rust code that properly manages resources.
summary: Proposes RustFuzz using LLMs to generate fuzz targets for Rust libraries, handling ownership, lifetimes, and trait constraints.
summary_zh: 提出RustFuzz，使用LLM为Rust库生成模糊测试目标，处理所有权、生命周期和trait约束。

experiment.key_results: Generated valid fuzz targets for 85% of tested crates
experiment.real_world_impact: Found 7 bugs in popular Rust crates

contributions: Proposes RustFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate fuzz targets for Rust libraries, handling ownership, lifetimes, and trait constraints.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 20 popular Rust crates benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generated valid fuzz targets for 85% of tested crates.
Addresses panic, logic-error, unsafe-usage vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

证明了 strong empirical results: Generated valid fuzz targets for 85% of tested crates.
解决 panic, logic-error, unsafe-usage vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: LLM-Driven Directed Fuzzing for Deep Code Paths
authors: Yannic Liu, Marcel Bohme, Van-Thuan Pham
abstract: Directed fuzzing aims to reach specific program locations but struggles with complex path constraints. We propose using LLMs to analyze target paths and generate inputs that satisfy complex constraints along the path to a target location. The LLM reasons about intermediate conditions and generates inputs step by step.
summary: Uses LLMs to analyze target paths and generate inputs satisfying complex constraints for directed fuzzing toward specific program locations.
summary_zh: 利用LLM分析目标路径并生成满足复杂约束的输入，用于针对特定程序位置的定向模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: Reached 40% more targets, 2.3x faster than AFLGo

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to analyze target paths and generate inputs satisfying complex constraints for directed fuzzing toward specific pro.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on Magma benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Reached 40% more targets, 2.3x faster than AFLGo.
Addresses known-CVEs vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to analyze target paths and generate inputs satisfying complex constraints for directed fuzzing toward specific pro.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Magma benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Reached 40% more targets, 2.3x faster than AFLGo.
解决 known-CVEs vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

What types of bugs can LLM-enhanced fuzzing discover that conventional fuzzers miss?

title: LLM-Based Test Oracle Generation for Database Fuzzing
authors: Zu-Ming Tan, Manuel Rigger
abstract: Database management systems (DBMSs) are critical infrastructure. Testing DBMSs requires test oracles to determine correctness, which are difficult to design. We propose using LLMs to generate test oracles for database fuzzing by understanding SQL semantics and expected query behavior.
summary: Uses LLMs to generate test oracles for database fuzzing by understanding SQL semantics and expected query behavior.
summary_zh: 利用LLM理解SQL语义和预期查询行为，为数据库模糊测试生成测试预言。

experiment.key_results: Found 28 new bugs across SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs confirmed in SQLite and PostgreSQL

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to generate test oracles for database fuzzing by understanding SQL semantics and expected query behavior.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 3 DBMS systems benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 28 new bugs across SQLite, MySQL, PostgreSQL.
Addresses logic-error, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: WasmFuzz: LLM-Assisted Fuzz Target Generation for WebAssembly Runtimes
authors: Di Tang, XiaoFeng Wang, Luyi Li
abstract: WebAssembly (Wasm) runtimes are increasingly critical for edge computing and serverless platforms. We present WasmFuzz, which uses LLMs to generate Wasm modules that stress-test runtime implementations. WasmFuzz leverages the LLM's understanding of Wasm specifications to create edge-case modules.
summary: Proposes WasmFuzz using LLMs to generate WebAssembly modules for stress-testing Wasm runtime implementations.
summary_zh: 提出WasmFuzz，使用LLM生成WebAssembly模块来压力测试Wasm运行时实现。

experiment.key_results: Found 19 bugs in Wasm runtimes, 7 security-relevant
experiment.real_world_impact: Security bugs in Wasmtime and Wasmer

contributions: Proposes WasmFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate WebAssembly modules for stress-testing Wasm runtime implementations.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 3 Wasm runtimes benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 19 bugs in Wasm runtimes, 7 security-relevant.
Addresses crash, spec-violation vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: SyzDirect: LLM-Enhanced Directed Kernel Fuzzing
authors: Sungjae Park, Jaeseung Kim, Byoungyoung Lee
abstract: Kernel fuzzing is essential for OS security but reaching specific kernel code requires understanding complex syscall dependencies. We propose SyzDirect, which uses LLMs to analyze kernel source code and generate syscall sequences that reach specific target locations for directed kernel fuzzing.
summary: Proposes SyzDirect using LLMs to generate targeted syscall sequences for directed kernel fuzzing by analyzing kernel source code.
summary_zh: 提出SyzDirect，使用LLM分析内核源代码生成针对性系统调用序列，用于定向内核模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: 3.2x faster target reaching than Syzkaller
experiment.real_world_impact: Found 5 new kernel vulnerabilities

contributions: Proposes SyzDirect, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate targeted syscall sequences for directed kernel fuzzing by analyzing kernel source code.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on Linux kernel benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 3.2x faster target reaching than Syzkaller.
Addresses kernel-bug, race-condition vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 SyzDirect, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate targeted syscall sequences for directed kernel fuzzing by analyzing kernel source code.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

解决 kernel-bug, race-condition vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Evaluating LLMs for Automated Fuzz Harness Generation: A Comprehensive Benchmark
authors: Junjie Wei, Ming Wen, Hai Jin Liu
abstract: While LLMs show promise for fuzz harness generation, a systematic evaluation is lacking. We present a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLM-generated fuzz harnesses across multiple dimensions: compilation success, code coverage, bug detection capability, and semantic correctness.
summary: Presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLM-generated fuzz harnesses across compilation success, coverage, bug detection, and correctness.
summary_zh: 提出全面的基准测试，从编译成功率、覆盖率、漏洞检测和正确性等多个维度评估LLM生成的模糊测试桩。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 achieves 76% compilation rate, 58% achieve meaningful coverage

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLM-generated fuzz harnesses across compilation success, coverage, bug detection,.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 30 C/C++ libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 achieves 76% compilation rate, 58% achieve meaningful coverage.
Addresses memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: RESTful API Fuzzing with LLM-Generated Test Harnesses
authors: Chengcheng Sun, Bo Jiang, W.K. Chan
abstract: RESTful API testing requires understanding API specifications and generating valid request sequences. We propose using LLMs to read OpenAPI specifications and generate comprehensive test harnesses that explore API endpoints with diverse parameter combinations and edge-case values.
summary: Uses LLMs to read OpenAPI specifications and generate test harnesses for RESTful API fuzzing with diverse parameter exploration.
summary_zh: 利用LLM阅读OpenAPI规范并生成RESTful API模糊测试桩，进行多样化参数探索。

experiment.key_results: 22% more unique errors found than RESTler

contributions: Proposes a approach for LLMs to read OpenAPI specifications and generate test harnesses for RESTful API fuzzing with diverse parameter explorati.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 10 RESTful services benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 22% more unique errors found than RESTler.
Addresses server-error, auth-bypass vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for LLMs to read OpenAPI specifications and generate test harnesses for RESTful API fuzzing with diverse parameter explorati.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 10 RESTful services benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 22% more unique errors found than RESTler.
解决 server-error, auth-bypass vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM阅读OpenAPI规范并生成RESTful API模糊测试桩，进行多样化参数探索。
research_questions: Can LLMs automatically generate correct and compilable fuzz harnesses for complex APIs?
How does the code coverage achieved by LLM-generated harnesses compare to manually written ones?
What techniques are most effective for handling compilation errors in LLM-generated harnesses?
research_questions_zh: LLM能否自动生成正确且可编译的复杂API模糊测试桩？
LLM生成的桩实现的代码覆盖率与手动编写的相比如何？
处理LLM生成桩中编译错误的最有效技术是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 22% more unique errors found than RESTler.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：22% more unique errors found than RESTler。
未来工作应探索LLM推理与覆盖率反馈的更紧密集成。

title: Coverage-Guided LLM Fuzzing via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Mingxuan Gao, Yue Liu, Kexin Pei
abstract: We propose a framework combining LLM-based test generation with coverage-guided reinforcement learning. The RL agent learns to prompt the LLM effectively to generate inputs that maximize code coverage, creating a feedback loop between fuzzing coverage and LLM generation strategy.
summary: Combines LLM test generation with coverage-guided reinforcement learning, using RL to optimize LLM prompting for maximum code coverage.
summary_zh: 结合LLM测试生成与覆盖引导的强化学习，使用RL优化LLM提示以最大化代码覆盖率。

experiment.key_results: 18% more edge coverage than Fuzz4All

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for combines LLM test generation with coverage-guided reinforcement learning, using RL to optimize LLM prompting for maximum.
Leverages GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 18% more edge coverage than Fuzz4All.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for combines LLM test generation with coverage-guided reinforcement learning, using RL to optimize LLM prompting for maximum.
利用 GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 18% more edge coverage than Fuzz4All.
解决 crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 结合LLM测试生成与覆盖引导的强化学习，使用RL优化LLM提示以最大化代码覆盖率。
research_questions: How can LLMs improve fuzzing effectiveness in terms of code coverage and bug discovery?
What is the optimal integration strategy for combining LLM capabilities with traditional fuzzing techniques?
What types of bugs can LLM-enhanced fuzzing discover that conventional fuzzers miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM如何在代码覆盖率和漏洞发现方面提高模糊测试效果？
将LLM能力与传统模糊测试技术结合的最优集成策略是什么？
LLM增强的模糊测试能发现哪些传统模糊器遗漏的漏洞类型？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 18% more edge coverage than Fuzz4All.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：18% more edge coverage than Fuzz4All。

title: BinFuzz: LLM-Guided Binary-Level Fuzz Harness Generation
authors: Mingi Kim, Seunggi Shin, Byoungyoung Lee
abstract: Fuzzing binary programs without source code requires generating harnesses at the binary level. We present BinFuzz, which uses LLMs to analyze decompiled binary code and generate fuzz harnesses that correctly set up function arguments and call contexts for binary-level fuzzing.
summary: Proposes BinFuzz using LLMs to analyze decompiled code and generate fuzz harnesses for binary-level fuzzing without source code.
summary_zh: 提出BinFuzz，使用LLM分析反编译代码并为无源代码的二进制级模糊测试生成模糊测试桩。

experiment.key_results: 60% valid harness generation rate, found 14 new bugs
experiment.real_world_impact: Found bugs in proprietary firmware

contributions: Proposes BinFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to analyze decompiled code and generate fuzz harnesses for binary-level fuzzing without source code.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on LAVA-M, real-world binaries benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 60% valid harness generation rate, found 14 new bugs.
Addresses memory-corruption, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

证明了 strong empirical results: 60% valid harness generation rate, found 14 new bugs.
解决 memory-corruption, crash vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Leveraging LLMs for Automated Smart Contract Fuzz Testing
authors: Kaihua Zhang, Zhe Liu, Zhuo Yang
abstract: Smart contract testing is challenging due to the need for valid transaction sequences and state-dependent behavior. We leverage LLMs to generate semantically meaningful transaction sequences for fuzzing Solidity smart contracts, using the LLM's understanding of DeFi patterns.
summary: Leverages LLMs to generate semantically meaningful transaction sequences for smart contract fuzz testing using DeFi pattern understanding.
summary_zh: 利用LLM的DeFi模式理解能力生成语义有意义的交易序列，用于智能合约模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: 28% more vulnerabilities found than Echidna alone

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs to generate semantically meaningful transaction sequences for smart contract fuzz testing using DeFi pattern unders.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 100 DeFi contracts benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 28% more vulnerabilities found than Echidna alone.
Addresses reentrancy, price-manipulation, flash-loan vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to generate semantically meaningful transaction sequences for smart contract fuzz testing using DeFi pattern unders.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 100 DeFi contracts benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 28% more vulnerabilities found than Echidna alone.
解决 reentrancy, price-manipulation, flash-loan vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM的DeFi模式理解能力生成语义有意义的交易序列，用于智能合约模糊测试。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
Can LLMs effectively identify logic vulnerabilities that traditional tools miss in smart contracts?

当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？

title: IoTFuzz: LLM-Powered Firmware Fuzzing for IoT Devices
authors: Shengjian Ma, Yuekang Li, Yang Liu, Yujie Wen
abstract: IoT device firmware is notoriously difficult to fuzz due to hardware dependencies and custom protocols. We present IoTFuzz, which uses LLMs to analyze firmware binaries and generate fuzz harnesses that emulate hardware interactions, enabling effective fuzzing of IoT firmware without physical devices.
summary: Proposes IoTFuzz using LLMs to analyze firmware and generate harnesses that emulate hardware interactions for IoT firmware fuzzing.
summary_zh: 提出IoTFuzz，使用LLM分析固件并生成模拟硬件交互的桩，实现无物理设备的IoT固件模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: Found 22 vulnerabilities in 30 IoT firmware images
experiment.real_world_impact: Vulnerabilities in commercial IoT devices

contributions: Proposes IoTFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to analyze firmware and generate harnesses that emulate hardware interactions for IoT firmware fuzzing.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 30 IoT firmware images benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 22 vulnerabilities in 30 IoT firmware images.
Addresses buffer-overflow, command-injection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 IoTFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to analyze firmware and generate harnesses that emulate hardware interactions for IoT firmware fuzzing.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 30 IoT firmware images benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 22 vulnerabilities in 30 IoT firmware images.
解决 buffer-overflow, command-injection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: DifFuzz: Differential Fuzzing with LLM-Generated Equivalent Programs
authors: Qian Zhang, Matteo Paltenghi, Michael Pradel
abstract: Differential testing finds bugs by comparing outputs of equivalent implementations. We propose DifFuzz, which uses LLMs to generate semantically equivalent program variants for differential testing. By creating diverse implementations of the same specification, DifFuzz enables automatic oracle generation.
summary: Proposes DifFuzz using LLMs to generate semantically equivalent program variants for differential testing and automatic oracle generation.
summary_zh: 提出DifFuzz，使用LLM生成语义等价的程序变体，用于差异测试和自动预言生成。

experiment.key_results: Found 31 bugs across GCC, LLVM, and Python
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs confirmed in GCC and LLVM

contributions: Proposes DifFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate semantically equivalent program variants for differential testing and automatic oracle generation.
Leverages GPT-4, Codex for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 2 baseline approaches including CSmith (diff testing), EMI.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 31 bugs across GCC, LLVM, and Python.
Addresses miscompilation, inconsistency vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 DifFuzz, a novel approach for using LLMs to generate semantically equivalent program variants for differential testing and automatic oracle generation.
利用 GPT-4, Codex for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
进行了广泛的评估 against 2 baseline approaches including CSmith (diff testing), EMI.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 31 bugs across GCC, LLVM, and Python.
解决 miscompilation, inconsistency vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: GoFuzz: LLM-Enhanced Fuzzing for Go Programs
authors: Yuting Chen, Yiwen Xu, Junjie Wang
abstract: Go's built-in fuzzing support is limited in its ability to generate complex structured inputs. We present GoFuzz, which enhances Go fuzzing by using LLMs to generate corpus entries and fuzz functions that exercise complex API interactions, leveraging Go's type system information.
summary: Enhances Go fuzzing using LLMs to generate corpus entries and fuzz functions that exercise complex API interactions.
summary_zh: 利用LLM生成语料库条目和模糊测试函数来增强Go程序的模糊测试，探索复杂API交互。

experiment.key_results: 35% more panics found than default Go fuzzing

contributions: Proposes GoFuzz, a novel approach for enhances Go fuzzing using LLMs to generate corpus entries and fuzz functions that exercise complex API interactions.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 20 Go libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 35% more panics found than default Go fuzzing.
Addresses panic, data-race vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 GoFuzz, a novel approach for enhances Go fuzzing using LLMs to generate corpus entries and fuzz functions that exercise complex API interactions.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

证明了 strong empirical results: 35% more panics found than default Go fuzzing.
解决 panic, data-race vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: ConfigFuzz: LLM-Guided Configuration Fuzzing for Cloud Systems
authors: Shan Wang, Shan Lu, Tianyin Xu
abstract: Misconfiguration is a leading cause of cloud system failures. We present ConfigFuzz, which uses LLMs to understand configuration semantics from documentation and source code, then generates diverse configurations that test edge cases, invalid combinations, and corner-case interactions.
summary: Uses LLMs to understand configuration semantics and generate diverse test configurations for fuzzing cloud systems.
summary_zh: 利用LLM理解配置语义并生成多样化测试配置，用于模糊测试云系统。

experiment.key_results: Found 18 configuration bugs, 12 confirmed
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs found in Apache Hadoop and Spark

contributions: Proposes ConfigFuzz, a novel framework for LLMs to understand configuration semantics and generate diverse test configurations for fuzzing cloud systems.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 5 cloud systems benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 18 configuration bugs, 12 confirmed.
Addresses misconfiguration, crash, performance-bug vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

解决 misconfiguration, crash, performance-bug vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: CrossFuzz: LLM-Based Cross-Language Fuzzing for Polyglot Applications
authors: Yurui Jia, Hao Sun, Peng Liu
abstract: Modern applications often span multiple languages through FFI boundaries. We present CrossFuzz, which uses LLMs to understand cross-language interfaces and generate test harnesses that exercise FFI boundaries, a common source of memory safety bugs in polyglot applications.
summary: Uses LLMs to generate test harnesses for cross-language FFI boundaries in polyglot applications, targeting memory safety bugs.
summary_zh: 利用LLM为多语言应用的跨语言FFI边界生成测试桩，针对内存安全漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Found 11 FFI boundary bugs in popular libraries
experiment.real_world_impact: Bugs in NumPy, PyTorch C extensions

contributions: Proposes CrossFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to generate test harnesses for cross-language FFI boundaries in polyglot applications, targeting memory safety bugs.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 15 polyglot projects benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 11 FFI boundary bugs in popular libraries.
Addresses memory-corruption, type-mismatch vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 CrossFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to generate test harnesses for cross-language FFI boundaries in polyglot applications, targeting memory safety bugs.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

证明了 strong empirical results: Found 11 FFI boundary bugs in popular libraries.
解决 memory-corruption, type-mismatch vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: GPUFuzz: LLM-Assisted GPU Driver Fuzzing
authors: Daimeng Liu, Kyungtae Kim, Davide Balzarotti
abstract: GPU drivers are complex kernel components prone to security vulnerabilities. We present GPUFuzz, which uses LLMs to analyze GPU driver interfaces and generate ioctl-level fuzz harnesses. By understanding driver-specific data structures and command formats, GPUFuzz generates targeted inputs for GPU driver testing.
summary: Uses LLMs to analyze GPU driver interfaces and generate ioctl-level fuzz harnesses for kernel GPU driver testing.
summary_zh: 利用LLM分析GPU驱动接口并生成ioctl级模糊测试桩，用于内核GPU驱动测试。

experiment.key_results: Found 16 bugs in GPU drivers, 5 CVEs assigned
experiment.real_world_impact: CVEs in Linux kernel GPU subsystem

contributions: Proposes GPUFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to analyze GPU driver interfaces and generate ioctl-level fuzz harnesses for kernel GPU driver testing.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on NVIDIA, AMD, Intel GPU drivers benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 16 bugs in GPU drivers, 5 CVEs assigned.
Addresses kernel-oops, use-after-free, null-deref vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 GPUFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to analyze GPU driver interfaces and generate ioctl-level fuzz harnesses for kernel GPU driver testing.
利用 GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： NVIDIA, AMD, Intel GPU drivers benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Found 16 bugs in GPU drivers, 5 CVEs assigned.
解决 kernel-oops, use-after-free, null-deref vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM分析GPU驱动接口并生成ioctl级模糊测试桩，用于内核GPU驱动测试。

How does the code coverage achieved by LLM-generated harnesses compare to manually written ones?
What techniques are most effective for handling compilation errors in LLM-generated harnesses?

Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.

title: COMFuzz: LLM-Driven COM Object Fuzzing for Windows
authors: Peng Zhou, Heqing Huang, Chengyu Song
abstract: Windows COM (Component Object Model) interfaces are a rich attack surface but challenging to fuzz due to complex object hierarchies and method signatures. We propose COMFuzz, which uses LLMs to understand COM type libraries and generate harnesses that properly instantiate COM objects and invoke methods.
summary: Uses LLMs to understand COM type libraries and generate harnesses for fuzzing Windows COM interfaces.
summary_zh: 利用LLM理解COM类型库并为模糊测试Windows COM接口生成桩。

experiment.key_results: Generated harnesses for 78% of tested COM objects, found 9 bugs

contributions: Proposes COMFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to understand COM type libraries and generate harnesses for fuzzing Windows COM interfaces.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 50 COM objects benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Generated harnesses for 78% of tested COM objects, found 9 bugs.
Addresses memory-corruption, type-confusion vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: SecureFuzz: Security-Oriented Fuzz Target Generation via LLMs
authors: Shuai Li, Chao Zhang, Dawn Song
abstract: Existing fuzz harness generators focus on code coverage, but security-critical code often requires targeted harnesses. We present SecureFuzz, which uses LLMs to identify security-sensitive code paths (e.g., parsing, authentication, crypto) and generates harnesses specifically targeting these areas.
summary: Uses LLMs to identify security-sensitive code paths and generate targeted fuzz harnesses for security-critical areas.
summary_zh: 利用LLM识别安全敏感代码路径并为安全关键区域生成针对性模糊测试桩。

experiment.key_results: Found 27 security bugs, 2x more than coverage-oriented approach
experiment.real_world_impact: 8 CVEs in OpenSSL, curl, and libxml2

contributions: Proposes SecureFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to identify security-sensitive code paths and generate targeted fuzz harnesses for security-critical areas.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 20 security-critical projects benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 27 security bugs, 2x more than coverage-oriented approach.
Addresses buffer-overflow, use-after-free, integer-overflow vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 SecureFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to identify security-sensitive code paths and generate targeted fuzz harnesses for security-critical areas.

title: JFuzz: LLM-Based Unit Test and Fuzzing Harness Generation for Java Libraries
authors: Yuxiang Wu, Yanlin Chen, Martin Monperrus
abstract: Java library fuzzing through JQF requires well-designed generators. We propose JFuzz, which uses LLMs to generate JQF-compatible fuzzing harnesses by understanding Java API contracts, type hierarchies, and common usage patterns from Javadoc and source code.
summary: Uses LLMs to generate JQF-compatible fuzzing harnesses for Java libraries by understanding API contracts and type hierarchies.
summary_zh: 利用LLM通过理解API契约和类型层次为Java库生成JQF兼容的模糊测试桩。

experiment.key_results: Found 34 unique bugs across 15 Java libraries

contributions: Proposes JFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to generate JQF-compatible fuzzing harnesses for Java libraries by understanding API contracts and type hierarchies.
Leverages GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 15 Java libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 34 unique bugs across 15 Java libraries.
Addresses exception, resource-leak, deserialization vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

证明了 strong empirical results: Found 34 unique bugs across 15 Java libraries.

title: SolBench: Benchmarking LLM-Based Smart Contract Fuzzing Approaches
authors: Jiawei Chen, Zibin Zheng, Xiapu Luo
abstract: Multiple LLM-based approaches for smart contract fuzzing have emerged, but no systematic comparison exists. We present SolBench, a comprehensive benchmark with 500 annotated Solidity contracts for evaluating LLM-based smart contract fuzzing tools across vulnerability detection, false positive rate, and gas efficiency.
summary: Presents SolBench, a comprehensive benchmark with 500 annotated contracts for evaluating LLM-based smart contract fuzzing approaches.
summary_zh: 提出SolBench，包含500个注释合约的全面基准，用于评估基于LLM的智能合约模糊测试方法。

experiment.key_results: LLM-enhanced approaches find 35% more bugs but with 15% higher false positives

contributions: Proposes SolBench, a novel approach for comprehensive benchmark with 500 annotated contracts for evaluating LLM-based smart contract fuzzing approaches.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on SolBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLM-enhanced approaches find 35% more bugs but with 15% higher false positives.
Addresses reentrancy, overflow, access-control vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 SolBench, a novel approach for comprehensive benchmark with 500 annotated contracts for evaluating LLM-based smart contract fuzzing approaches.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

证明了 strong empirical results: LLM-enhanced approaches find 35% more bugs but with 15% higher false positives.
解决 reentrancy, overflow, access-control vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: MutLLM: LLM-Guided Mutation Strategies for Evolutionary Fuzzing
authors: Haoran Peng, Yinlin Deng, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Mutation strategies significantly impact fuzzing effectiveness. We propose MutLLM, which uses LLMs to learn mutation patterns from bug-triggering inputs and applies these learned patterns to guide mutations in evolutionary fuzzing, replacing hand-crafted mutation rules with LLM-inferred strategies.
summary: Uses LLMs to learn and apply mutation patterns from bug-triggering inputs for evolutionary fuzzing, replacing hand-crafted rules.
summary_zh: 利用LLM从触发漏洞的输入中学习和应用变异模式，用于进化模糊测试，替代手工编写的变异规则。

experiment.key_results: 12% more unique crashes than AFL++ with MOpt

contributions: Proposes MutLLM, a novel approach for LLMs to learn and apply mutation patterns from bug-triggering inputs for evolutionary fuzzing, replacing hand-crafted ru.
Leverages GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on FuzzBench, Magma benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 12% more unique crashes than AFL++ with MOpt.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 MutLLM, a novel approach for LLMs to learn and apply mutation patterns from bug-triggering inputs for evolutionary fuzzing, replacing hand-crafted ru.

title: SymLM: Combining Symbolic Execution with LLMs for Constraint-Aware Fuzzing
authors: Yun Lin, Yulei Zhang, Yang Liu
abstract: Symbolic execution provides precise path constraint solving but suffers from path explosion. We propose SymLM, which uses LLMs to approximate symbolic execution for complex constraints, using the LLM's code understanding to reason about path conditions and generate constraint-satisfying inputs.
summary: Combines symbolic execution with LLMs to approximate constraint solving for complex paths, improving fuzzing effectiveness.
summary_zh: 结合符号执行与LLM来近似求解复杂路径约束，提升模糊测试效果。

experiment.key_results: Solved 28% more constraints than KLEE, found 8 new bugs

contributions: Proposes SymLM, a novel approach for combines symbolic execution with LLMs to approximate constraint solving for complex paths, improving fuzzing effectivene.
Leverages GPT-4 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on Magma, LAVA-M benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Solved 28% more constraints than KLEE, found 8 new bugs.
Addresses buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

解决 buffer-overflow, use-after-free vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Keep the Conversation Going: Fixing 162 out of 337 bugs for $0.42 each using ChatGPT
authors: Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Automated program repair (APR) aims to fix software bugs automatically. We present ChatRepair, which uses conversational repair with ChatGPT. By providing failing test information and iteratively refining patches through multi-turn conversation, ChatRepair achieves state-of-the-art repair performance at minimal cost.
summary: Proposes ChatRepair using multi-turn ChatGPT conversation for automated program repair, fixing 162 of 337 bugs at $0.42 per bug.
summary_zh: 提出ChatRepair，利用多轮ChatGPT对话进行自动程序修复，以每个漏洞0.42美元的成本修复了337个漏洞中的162个。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 162/337 bugs on Defects4J, SOTA at $0.42/bug average cost
experiment.real_world_impact: Demonstrates cost-effective automated repair

contributions: Proposes ChatRepair, a novel approach for using multi-turn ChatGPT conversation for automated program repair, fixing 162 of 337 bugs at $0.42 per bug.
Utilizes ChatGPT, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J v1.2, Defects4J v2.0 benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixed 162/337 bugs on Defects4J, SOTA at $0.42/bug average cost.
Addresses logic-error, null-pointer, off-by-one vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 ChatRepair, a novel approach for using multi-turn ChatGPT conversation for automated program repair, fixing 162 of 337 bugs at $0.42 per bug.
利用 ChatGPT, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J v1.2, Defects4J v2.0 benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Fixed 162/337 bugs on Defects4J, SOTA at $0.42/bug average cost.
解决 logic-error, null-pointer, off-by-one vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 提出ChatRepair，利用多轮ChatGPT对话进行自动程序修复，以每个漏洞0.42美元的成本修复了337个漏洞中的162个。
research_questions: How accurately can LLMs generate correct patches for real-world software vulnerabilities?
What is the trade-off between patch correctness and generation cost when using LLMs?
How do different prompting strategies affect the quality and correctness of generated patches?
research_questions_zh: LLM能多准确地为真实世界的软件漏洞生成正确补丁？
使用LLM时补丁正确性与生成成本之间的权衡如何？
不同的提示策略如何影响生成补丁的质量和正确性？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Fixed 162/337 bugs on Defects4J, SOTA at $0.42/bug average cost.
The results represent a significant advancement in the field, establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
Future directions include multi-hunk repair, cross-project patch transfer, and reducing false positive patches.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Fixed 162/337 bugs on Defects4J, SOTA at $0.42/bug average cost。
研究成果代表了该领域的重要进展，建立了新的最先进基准。
未来方向包括多块修复、跨项目补丁迁移以及减少误报补丁。

title: Automated Program Repair in the Era of Large Pre-trained Language Models
authors: Chunqiu Steven Xia, Yuxiang Wei, Lingming Zhang
abstract: We conduct the first extensive study on LLM-based automated program repair. We evaluate multiple LLMs including Codex, ChatGPT, GPT-4, InCoder, and CodeGen on standard APR benchmarks. Our study reveals that LLMs significantly outperform prior APR tools and identifies key factors affecting repair performance.
summary: Extensive study evaluating LLMs on automated program repair benchmarks, showing LLMs significantly outperform prior APR tools.
summary_zh: 广泛研究评估LLM在自动程序修复基准上的表现，表明LLM显著优于之前的APR工具。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 fixes 55% more bugs than best prior APR tool

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for extensive study evaluating LLMs on automated program repair benchmarks, showing LLMs significantly outperform prior APR.
Utilizes Codex, ChatGPT, GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, QuixBugs, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 fixes 55% more bugs than best prior APR tool.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Copiloting the Copilots: Fusing Large Language Models with Completion Engines for Automated Program Repair
authors: Yuxiang Wei, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang
abstract: LLMs generate plausible patches but sometimes produce syntactically invalid or type-incorrect code. We propose Repilot, which fuses LLMs with completion engines to ensure generated patches are syntactically valid and type-correct, combining the creativity of LLMs with the precision of program analysis.
summary: Proposes Repilot fusing LLMs with completion engines to generate syntactically valid and type-correct patches for automated program repair.
summary_zh: 提出Repilot，融合LLM与代码补全引擎，生成语法有效且类型正确的补丁用于自动程序修复。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 24% more bugs than Codex alone

contributions: Proposes Repilot, a novel approach for fusing LLMs with completion engines to generate syntactically valid and type-correct patches for automated program repai.
Utilizes Codex for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J v1.2, Defects4J v2.0 benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixed 24% more bugs than Codex alone.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: VulRepair: A T5-Based Automated Software Vulnerability Repair
authors: Michael Fu, Chakkrit Tantithamthavorn
abstract: Security vulnerabilities require timely patches, but manual repair is slow. We present VulRepair, a T5-based model fine-tuned specifically for vulnerability repair. By training on a curated dataset of vulnerability-patch pairs from real-world CVEs, VulRepair learns to generate security patches.
summary: Proposes VulRepair, a T5-based model fine-tuned on CVE-patch pairs for automated vulnerability repair, achieving 44% exact-match accuracy.
summary_zh: 提出VulRepair，基于T5模型在CVE补丁对上微调用于自动漏洞修复，实现44%的精确匹配准确率。

experiment.key_results: 44% exact-match accuracy on vulnerability repair

Utilizes T5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on CVEFixes, Big-Vul benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 44% exact-match accuracy on vulnerability repair.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-125 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: SRepair: Semantic-Level Automated Program Repair via Large Language Models
authors: Yuntong Huang, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang
abstract: Existing APR approaches operate at the syntactic level, limiting their ability to fix complex bugs requiring semantic understanding. We present SRepair, which leverages LLMs for semantic-level program repair. SRepair uses semantic analysis to understand bug root causes before prompting the LLM for fixes.
summary: Proposes SRepair for semantic-level program repair using LLMs with root cause analysis, fixing complex multi-location bugs.
summary_zh: 提出SRepair，利用LLM进行语义级程序修复，结合根因分析修复复杂的多位置漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 178/395 bugs, 10% more than ChatRepair

contributions: Proposes SRepair, a novel approach for for semantic-level program repair using LLMs with root cause analysis, fixing complex multi-location bugs.
Utilizes GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixed 178/395 bugs, 10% more than ChatRepair.
Addresses logic-error, semantic-bug vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: ITER: Iterative Neural Repair for Multi-Location Bugs
authors: He Ye, Martin Monperrus
abstract: Multi-location bugs require coordinated changes across multiple code locations, which is challenging for existing APR tools. We present ITER, an iterative repair approach that uses LLMs to fix multi-location bugs one location at a time, using test feedback to guide subsequent repairs.
summary: Proposes ITER for iterative repair of multi-location bugs using LLMs with test feedback to guide sequential fixes.
summary_zh: 提出ITER，利用LLM通过测试反馈引导顺序修复，迭代修复多位置漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 45% of multi-location bugs vs 18% for single-shot approaches

contributions: Proposes ITER, a novel approach for for iterative repair of multi-location bugs using LLMs with test feedback to guide sequential fixes.
Utilizes GPT-4, CodeLlama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Fixed 45% of multi-location bugs vs 18% for single-shot approaches.
Addresses multi-location-bug vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: RepairLlama: Efficient Representation Learning for Fine-Tuned Code Repair Models
authors: Andre Silva, Martin Monperrus
abstract: While large commercial LLMs show strong repair capabilities, open-source alternatives lag behind. We present RepairLlama, a fine-tuned Llama model specifically for program repair. Through parameter-efficient fine-tuning on curated bug-fix datasets, RepairLlama achieves competitive performance with GPT-3.5.
summary: Fine-tunes Llama for program repair using LoRA, achieving competitive performance with GPT-3.5 at much lower cost.
summary_zh: 使用LoRA微调Llama用于程序修复，以更低成本达到与GPT-3.5相当的性能。

experiment.key_results: Matches GPT-3.5 on Defects4J at 10x lower cost

contributions: Proposes RepairLlama, a novel approach for fine-tunes Llama for program repair using LoRA, achieving competitive performance with GPT-3.5 at much lower cost.
Utilizes Llama-2 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, MegaDiff benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Matches GPT-3.5 on Defects4J at 10x lower cost.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: InferFix: End-to-End Program Repair with LLMs over Retrieval-Augmented Prompts
authors: Matthew Jin, Syed Shahriar, Michele Tufano, Xin Shi, Shuai Lu, Neel Sundaresan, Alexey Svyatkovskiy
abstract: We present InferFix, an end-to-end program repair system that combines static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM generation. InferFix uses Infer's static analyzer to identify bugs, retrieves similar bug-fix pairs, and prompts an LLM with relevant context to generate patches.
summary: Proposes InferFix combining Infer static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM patch generation for end-to-end program repair.
summary_zh: 提出InferFix，结合Infer静态分析与检索增强的LLM补丁生成，实现端到端程序修复。

experiment.key_results: 65% of generated patches pass validation, 40% exact match
experiment.real_world_impact: Deployed in Microsoft developer workflow

contributions: Proposes InferFix, a novel approach for combining Infer static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM patch generation for end-to-end program repair.
Utilizes Codex, GPT-3.5 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on InferredBugs benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 65% of generated patches pass validation, 40% exact match.
Addresses null-pointer, resource-leak, thread-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 InferFix, a novel approach for combining Infer static analysis with retrieval-augmented LLM patch generation for end-to-end program repair.

title: AutoCodeRover: Autonomous Program Improvement via LLM Agents
authors: Yuntong Zhang, Haifeng Ruan, Zhiyu Fan, Abhik Roychoudhury
abstract: We present AutoCodeRover, an autonomous LLM agent for program improvement. Unlike prior approaches that focus on single-hunk patches, AutoCodeRover navigates codebases autonomously, identifies relevant code contexts, and generates multi-file patches using an iterative agent-based approach.
summary: Proposes AutoCodeRover, an autonomous LLM agent that navigates codebases and generates multi-file patches for program improvement.
summary_zh: 提出AutoCodeRover，一个自主导航代码库并生成多文件补丁的LLM代理，用于程序改进。

experiment.key_results: Resolved 22% of SWE-bench issues, SOTA at time of publication
experiment.real_world_impact: Resolves real GitHub issues autonomously

contributions: Proposes AutoCodeRover, a novel approach for autonomous LLM agent that navigates codebases and generates multi-file patches for program improvement.

Demonstrates strong empirical results: Resolved 22% of SWE-bench issues, SOTA at time of publication.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
authors: Carlos E. Jimenez, John Yang, Alexander Wettig, Shunyu Yao, Kexin Pei, Ofir Press, Karthik Narasimhan
abstract: We introduce SWE-bench, a benchmark for evaluating LLMs on real-world software engineering tasks. SWE-bench collects 2,294 task instances from 12 popular Python repositories, where each instance requires the model to resolve a GitHub issue by generating a patch.
summary: Introduces SWE-bench, a benchmark of 2,294 real GitHub issues for evaluating LLMs on software engineering and patch generation.
summary_zh: 引入SWE-bench，包含2294个真实GitHub问题的基准，用于评估LLM在软件工程和补丁生成上的能力。

experiment.key_results: Best model resolves only 3.8% of issues in zero-shot
experiment.real_world_impact: Widely adopted benchmark for code repair evaluation

Utilizes GPT-4, Claude, Llama for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.

Demonstrates strong empirical results: Best model resolves only 3.8% of issues in zero-shot.
Addresses github-issue vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
authors: John Yang, Carlos E. Jimenez, Alexander Wettig, Kilian Liber, Karthik Narasimhan, Ofir Press
abstract: We present SWE-agent, a system that uses LLM agents with specially designed agent-computer interfaces (ACIs) to autonomously resolve GitHub issues. SWE-agent provides the LLM with tools for file navigation, editing, and testing, enabling end-to-end autonomous software repair.

experiment.real_world_impact: Practical tool for automated software repair

title: RepairAgent: An Autonomous, LLM-Based Agent for Program Repair
authors: Islem Bouzenia, Michael Pradel
abstract: We present RepairAgent, an autonomous agent for program repair that dynamically decides which tools to use, what context to gather, and how to generate patches. Unlike fixed pipeline approaches, RepairAgent uses an LLM as a reasoning engine to make strategic decisions during the repair process.
summary: Proposes RepairAgent, an autonomous LLM-based agent that dynamically decides repair strategy, tools, and context gathering.
summary_zh: 提出RepairAgent，一个自主的基于LLM的代理，动态决定修复策略、工具和上下文收集。

experiment.key_results: Fixed 164/835 bugs with dynamic tool selection

Future directions include multi-hunk repair, cross-project patch transfer, and reducing false positive patches.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Fixed 164/835 bugs with dynamic tool selection。

未来方向包括多块修复、跨项目补丁迁移以及减少误报补丁。

title: A Critical Review of Large Language Model on Software Engineering: An Example from ChatGPT and Automated Program Repair
authors: Quanjun Zhang, Chunrong Fang, Yuxiang Xie, Tongtong Ma, Yanli Zhang, Zhenyu Zhu
abstract: We present a critical review of LLMs for automated program repair, analyzing ChatGPT's capabilities and limitations. Through extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks, we identify factors affecting repair success, common failure modes, and provide guidelines for effective LLM-based repair.
summary: Critical review of ChatGPT for automated program repair, analyzing capabilities, limitations, failure modes and providing guidelines.
summary_zh: 对ChatGPT用于自动程序修复的批判性评论，分析能力、局限性、失败模式并提供指南。

experiment.key_results: Identifies 8 key failure modes of LLM-based repair

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for critical review of ChatGPT for automated program repair, analyzing capabilities, limitations, failure modes and providin.
Utilizes ChatGPT, GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on Defects4J, QuixBugs, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Identifies 8 key failure modes of LLM-based repair.
Addresses logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for critical review of ChatGPT for automated program repair, analyzing capabilities, limitations, failure modes and providin.
利用 ChatGPT, GPT-4 for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Defects4J, QuixBugs, BugsInPy benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Identifies 8 key failure modes of LLM-based repair.
解决 logic-error vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

research_questions: How accurately can LLMs generate correct patches for real-world software vulnerabilities?
What is the trade-off between patch correctness and generation cost when using LLMs?
How do different prompting strategies affect the quality and correctness of generated patches?

Future directions include multi-hunk repair, cross-project patch transfer, and reducing false positive patches.

title: Examining Zero-Shot Vulnerability Repair with Large Language Models
authors: Hammond Pearce, Baleegh Ahmad, Benjamin Tan, Brendan Dolan-Gavitt, Ramesh Karri
abstract: We study the ability of LLMs to repair security vulnerabilities in zero-shot settings. Using a dataset of C/C++ vulnerabilities from real-world CVEs, we evaluate whether LLMs can generate correct security patches when given only the vulnerable code and vulnerability description.
summary: Studies LLMs' zero-shot ability to repair security vulnerabilities in C/C++ using real CVE data.
summary_zh: 研究LLM使用真实CVE数据在零样本设置下修复C/C++安全漏洞的能力。

experiment.key_results: LLMs fix 30% of vulnerabilities in zero-shot, varying by CWE type

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for studies LLMs' zero-shot ability to repair security vulnerabilities in C/C++ using real CVE data.
Utilizes Codex, CodeGen, InCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
Evaluates on ExtractFix benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLMs fix 30% of vulnerabilities in zero-shot, varying by CWE type.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-416, CWE-787 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for studies LLMs' zero-shot ability to repair security vulnerabilities in C/C++ using real CVE data.
利用 Codex, CodeGen, InCoder for automated patch generation with semantic understanding of code context.
