Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 75% of known firmware vulnerabilities.
Addresses command-injection, buffer-overflow, hardcoded-credentials vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

利用 GPT-4, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: Smart Contract Vulnerability Detection via LLM-Based Symbolic Reasoning
authors: Jie Chen, Wuqi Liu, Yanlin Wang, Zibin Zheng
abstract: Smart contract vulnerability detection requires reasoning about complex transaction flows. We combine LLMs' natural language understanding with symbolic reasoning to detect smart contract vulnerabilities, outperforming both pure ML and pure formal methods.
summary: Combines LLM understanding with symbolic reasoning for smart contract vulnerability detection.
summary_zh: 结合LLM理解与符号推理进行智能合约漏洞检测。

experiment.key_results: Combined approach achieves 92% precision vs 75% for GPT-4 alone

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for combines LLM understanding with symbolic reasoning for smart contract vulnerability detection.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on SmartBugs benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Combined approach achieves 92% precision vs 75% for GPT-4 alone.
Addresses reentrancy, integer-overflow, timestamp-dependence vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for combines LLM understanding with symbolic reasoning for smart contract vulnerability detection.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: LLM-Powered Supply Chain Vulnerability Detection in Open-Source Dependencies
authors: Yue Wang, Zhi Li, Jun Chen
abstract: Software supply chain attacks exploit vulnerabilities in open-source dependencies. We use LLMs to analyze dependency code changes, detect suspicious modifications, and identify potential supply chain attacks by understanding the semantic intent of code changes.

summary_zh: 利用LLM分析依赖代码变更的语义意图来检测供应链攻击。

experiment.key_results: Detects 88% of known malicious packages with 5% FPR

Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Backstabber's Knife, Known malicious packages benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 88% of known malicious packages with 5% FPR.
Addresses supply-chain, typosquatting, code-injection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs to detect supply chain attacks by analyzing semantic intent of dependency code changes.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: LLM-Based Android Application Vulnerability Detection
authors: Hisham Omar, Sultan Alqahtani, Antonio Bianchi
abstract: Android applications contain unique vulnerability classes including intent injection, content provider leaks, and insecure data storage. We evaluate LLMs' ability to detect Android-specific vulnerabilities by analyzing Java/Kotlin source code and manifest files.

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 detects 78% of Android vulnerabilities, complementary to FlowDroid

Addresses intent-injection, content-provider-leak, insecure-storage vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: PhishLLM: LLM-Based Phishing Detection and Analysis
authors: Mohammed Alqarni, Ahmed Nabulsi, Peng Liu
abstract: Phishing attacks continue to evolve rapidly. We present PhishLLM, which uses LLMs to analyze phishing emails and websites, detecting social engineering patterns, brand impersonation, and URL manipulation that evade traditional detection methods.
summary: Uses LLMs to detect phishing through analysis of social engineering patterns, brand impersonation, and URL manipulation.

contributions: Proposes PhishLLM, a novel approach for LLMs to detect phishing through analysis of social engineering patterns, brand impersonation, and URL manipulation.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: Automated Security Audit with LLMs: A Practical Evaluation
authors: Qiushi Wang, Hao Li, Chaoyang Xiao
abstract: We evaluate LLMs for automated security auditing of production codebases. Our study covers 10 real-world applications and evaluates the practicality of using LLMs as security auditors, measuring detection quality, effort reduction, and auditor trust.
summary: Evaluates LLMs for automated security auditing of 10 real-world applications, measuring quality and effort reduction.
summary_zh: 评估LLM对10个真实应用的自动安全审计，衡量质量和工作量减少。

experiment.key_results: LLM audit finds 60% of vulnerabilities found by human auditors, reduces time by 70%

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs for automated security auditing of 10 real-world applications, measuring quality and effort reduction.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on 10 production apps benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: LLM audit finds 60% of vulnerabilities found by human auditors, reduces time by 70%.
Addresses various vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs for automated security auditing of 10 real-world applications, measuring quality and effort reduction.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: CodeGuard: LLM-Powered Code Review for Security
authors: Xian Yang, Jian Chen, Xiang Li
abstract: Security-focused code review is essential but time-consuming. CodeGuard uses LLMs to automatically review code changes for security issues, providing detailed explanations and fix suggestions for detected vulnerabilities in pull requests.
summary: CodeGuard uses LLMs for automated security code review of pull requests with explanations and fix suggestions.
summary_zh: CodeGuard使用LLM对拉取请求进行自动安全代码审查，提供解释和修复建议。

experiment.key_results: Detects 72% of security issues in PRs, 85% developer satisfaction

contributions: Proposes CodeGuard, a novel approach for uses LLMs for automated security code review of pull requests with explanations and fix suggestions.

Evaluates on GitHub PRs benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 72% of security issues in PRs, 85% developer satisfaction.
Addresses injection, auth, crypto vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: LLMs for Software Composition Analysis: Detecting Vulnerable Dependencies
authors: Zhuotao Liu, Yufei Wu, Hao Xiao
abstract: Software Composition Analysis (SCA) detects vulnerable dependencies but relies on known vulnerability databases. We use LLMs to analyze dependency code for potential vulnerabilities even before they are reported, providing proactive security analysis.
summary: Uses LLMs for proactive SCA, detecting potential dependency vulnerabilities before they appear in CVE databases.
summary_zh: 利用LLM进行前瞻性SCA，在CVE数据库收录之前检测潜在依赖漏洞。

experiment.key_results: Identifies 25% of vulnerabilities before CVE disclosure

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs for proactive SCA, detecting potential dependency vulnerabilities before they appear in CVE databases.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Maven Central, npm registry benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Identifies 25% of vulnerabilities before CVE disclosure.
Addresses dependency-vulnerability vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: DeepSec: Deep Learning for Security Vulnerability Detection in Source Code
authors: Guang Chen, Kexin Pei, Chao Zhang
abstract: We present DeepSec, a comprehensive framework combining multiple deep learning architectures for vulnerability detection. DeepSec uses graph neural networks, transformers, and cross-modal fusion to capture both structural and semantic patterns in code.

title: Can LLMs Solve Capture-The-Flag Challenges?
authors: Hao Zhang, Xun Yang, Yuan Shi
abstract: CTF challenges test diverse security skills. We evaluate LLMs' ability to solve CTF challenges across categories: web exploitation, binary exploitation, cryptography, reverse engineering, and forensics, providing insights into LLMs' security reasoning capabilities.
summary: Evaluates LLMs solving CTF challenges across web, binary, crypto, reverse engineering, and forensics categories.
summary_zh: 评估LLM在Web、二进制、密码学、逆向工程和取证类别的CTF挑战中的解题能力。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 solves 47% of easy, 18% of medium, 3% of hard CTF challenges

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs solving CTF challenges across web, binary, crypto, reverse engineering, and forensics categories.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on picoCTF, HackTheBox benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 solves 47% of easy, 18% of medium, 3% of hard CTF challenges.
Addresses web, pwn, crypto vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs solving CTF challenges across web, binary, crypto, reverse engineering, and forensics categories.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?

title: Detecting Real-World Vulnerabilities with LLM-Guided Static Analysis
authors: Xin Wen, Yihe Li, Bo Chen
abstract: Traditional static analysis suffers from high false positive rates. We propose LLM-guided static analysis where the LLM acts as a filter and reasoning engine to reduce false positives from static analysis tools, achieving higher precision without sacrificing recall.
summary: LLM-guided static analysis using LLMs to reduce false positives from static analysis tools.
summary_zh: LLM引导的静态分析，利用LLM减少静态分析工具的误报。

experiment.key_results: Reduces false positives by 55% while maintaining 95% of true positives

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLM-guided static analysis using LLMs to reduce false positives from static analysis tools.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Real projects benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Reduces false positives by 55% while maintaining 95% of true positives.
Addresses null-pointer, resource-leak, buffer-overflow vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLM-guided static analysis using LLMs to reduce false positives from static analysis tools.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: CryptoLLM: Detecting Cryptographic Misuse with Large Language Models
authors: Wei Zhou, Sen Chen, Yinzhi Xue
abstract: Cryptographic misuse leads to severe security vulnerabilities. We present CryptoLLM, which uses LLMs to detect cryptographic API misuse by understanding crypto API semantics, parameter constraints, and common anti-patterns in cryptographic code.
summary: Uses LLMs to detect cryptographic API misuse by understanding crypto semantics and common anti-patterns.
summary_zh: 利用LLM理解密码学语义和常见反模式来检测加密API误用。

experiment.key_results: Detects 90% of crypto misuse with 8% FPR

contributions: Proposes CryptoLLM, a novel approach for LLMs to detect cryptographic API misuse by understanding crypto semantics and common anti-patterns.
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on CryptoAPI-Bench, CogniCrypt rules benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Detects 90% of crypto misuse with 8% FPR.
Addresses weak-crypto, insecure-random, hardcoded-key vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 CryptoLLM, a novel approach for LLMs to detect cryptographic API misuse by understanding crypto semantics and common anti-patterns.
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: IaC-Scan: LLM-Based Security Analysis of Infrastructure-as-Code
authors: Peng Wang, Akond Rahman, Laurie Williams
abstract: Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) scripts contain security misconfigurations that can compromise cloud infrastructure. We use LLMs to analyze Terraform, CloudFormation, and Kubernetes manifests for security issues, understanding cloud security best practices.
summary: Uses LLMs to detect security misconfigurations in IaC scripts (Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes).
summary_zh: 利用LLM检测IaC脚本（Terraform、CloudFormation、Kubernetes）中的安全配置错误。

experiment.key_results: Finds 30% more misconfigs than Checkov while explaining impact

contributions: Proposes IaC-Scan, a novel approach for LLMs to detect security misconfigurations in IaC scripts (Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes).
Leverages GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Real IaC repos benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Finds 30% more misconfigs than Checkov while explaining impact.
Addresses misconfiguration, overprivileged, exposed-secret vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 IaC-Scan, a novel approach for LLMs to detect security misconfigurations in IaC scripts (Terraform, CloudFormation, Kubernetes).
利用 GPT-4 for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

解决 misconfiguration, overprivileged, exposed-secret vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Extracting Training Data from Large Language Models
authors: Nicholas Carlini, Florian Tramer, Eric Wallace, Matthew Jagielski, Ariel Herbert-Voss, Katherine Lee, Adam Roberts, Tom Brown, Dawn Song, Ulfar Erlingsson
abstract: We demonstrate that an adversary can extract verbatim training data from GPT-2, including personally identifiable information, code, and URLs. Our attack exploits the model's memorization of training examples.
summary: Demonstrates extraction of verbatim training data from GPT-2, including PII and code, exploiting memorization.
summary_zh: 展示从GPT-2中提取逐字训练数据（包括PII和代码），利用模型记忆特性。

experiment.key_results: Extracted 600+ unique memorized training examples from GPT-2
experiment.real_world_impact: Foundational work on LLM privacy risks

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for demonstrates extraction of verbatim training data from GPT-2, including PII and code, exploiting memorization.
Applies GPT-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on GPT-2 training data benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Extracted 600+ unique memorized training examples from GPT-2.
Addresses data-extraction, memorization vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for demonstrates extraction of verbatim training data from GPT-2, including PII and code, exploiting memorization.
应用 GPT-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
在以下数据集上进行评估： GPT-2 training data benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Extracted 600+ unique memorized training examples from GPT-2.
解决 data-extraction, memorization vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 展示从GPT-2中提取逐字训练数据（包括PII和代码），利用模型记忆特性。
research_questions: What are the primary privacy risks associated with deploying LLMs in security-sensitive applications?
How effective are current defense mechanisms against privacy attacks on LLMs?
How can privacy-preserving techniques be applied without significantly degrading model performance?
research_questions_zh: 在安全敏感应用中部署LLM的主要隐私风险是什么？
当前防御机制对LLM隐私攻击的有效性如何？
如何在不显著降低模型性能的情况下应用隐私保护技术？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Extracted 600+ unique memorized training examples from GPT-2.
The results represent a significant advancement in the field, establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
Further research is needed on scalable privacy-preserving techniques that maintain model utility.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：Extracted 600+ unique memorized training examples from GPT-2。
研究成果代表了该领域的重要进展，建立了新的最先进基准。
需要进一步研究在保持模型效用的同时实现可扩展的隐私保护技术。

title: Quantifying Memorization Across Neural Language Models
authors: Nicholas Carlini, Daphne Ippolito, Matthew Jagielski, Katherine Lee, Florian Tramer, Chiyuan Zhang
abstract: We quantify memorization in language models, studying how memorization scales with model size, data duplication, and training time. Larger models memorize significantly more, with memorization scaling log-linearly with model size.
summary: Quantifies LLM memorization scaling: larger models memorize more, log-linear with model size.
summary_zh: 量化LLM记忆缩放规律：更大模型记忆更多，与模型大小呈对数线性关系。

experiment.key_results: Memorization scales log-linearly with model size, 10x model = 2x memorization

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for quantifies LLM memorization scaling: larger models memorize more, log-linear with model size.
Applies GPT-Neo, Pythia, LLaMA to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on The Pile benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Memorization scales log-linearly with model size, 10x model = 2x memorization.
Addresses memorization vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for quantifies LLM memorization scaling: larger models memorize more, log-linear with model size.
应用 GPT-Neo, Pythia, LLaMA to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
在以下数据集上进行评估： The Pile benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: Memorization scales log-linearly with model size, 10x model = 2x memorization.
解决 memorization vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: A Comprehensive Survey of Jailbreak Attacks and Defenses on Large Language Models
authors: Sibo Li, Huanran Chen, Xiao Yang
abstract: This survey comprehensively reviews jailbreak attacks on LLMs that bypass safety alignment, covering attack techniques, defense mechanisms, evaluation benchmarks, and future research directions.
summary: Comprehensive survey of jailbreak attacks on LLMs, covering attack techniques, defenses, and benchmarks.
summary_zh: LLM越狱攻击的全面综述，涵盖攻击技术、防御机制和基准。

experiment.key_results: Catalogs 200+ jailbreak techniques and 50+ defense mechanisms

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comprehensive survey of jailbreak attacks on LLMs, covering attack techniques, defenses, and benchmarks.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Catalogs 200+ jailbreak techniques and 50+ defense mechanisms.
Addresses jailbreak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for comprehensive survey of jailbreak attacks on LLMs, covering attack techniques, defenses, and benchmarks.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Catalogs 200+ jailbreak techniques and 50+ defense mechanisms.
解决 jailbreak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: LLM越狱攻击的全面综述，涵盖攻击技术、防御机制和基准。
research_questions: What are the primary privacy risks associated with deploying LLMs in security-sensitive applications?

title: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
authors: Andy Zou, Zifan Wang, J. Zico Kolter, Matt Fredrikson
abstract: We propose an automated method to generate adversarial suffixes that cause aligned LLMs to produce harmful content. These suffixes transfer across different LLMs, demonstrating a universal vulnerability in alignment-based safety mechanisms.
summary: Generates universal adversarial suffixes that bypass LLM safety alignment, transferable across different models.
summary_zh: 生成通用对抗性后缀绕过LLM安全对齐，可在不同模型间迁移。

experiment.key_results: 99.9% attack success on open-source, 84% on GPT-3.5, 47% on GPT-4
experiment.real_world_impact: Led to improved safety training across LLM providers

contributions: Proposes GCG, a novel approach for generates universal adversarial suffixes that bypass LLM safety alignment, transferable across different models.
Applies Llama-2, Vicuna, GPT-3.5 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on AdvBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 99.9% attack success on open-source, 84% on GPT-3.5, 47% on GPT-4.
Addresses adversarial-attack, jailbreak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 GCG, a novel approach for generates universal adversarial suffixes that bypass LLM safety alignment, transferable across different models.
应用 Llama-2, Vicuna, GPT-3.5 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

title: Red Teaming Language Models with Language Models
authors: Ethan Perez, Saffron Huang, Francis Song, Trevor Cai, Roman Ring, John Aslanides, Amelia Glaese, Nat McAleese, Geoffrey Irving
abstract: We use language models to automatically generate test cases for red teaming other language models. By prompting an LLM to generate adversarial inputs, we discover a wide range of offensive outputs, data leakage, and safety failures.
summary: Uses LLMs to automatically red-team other LLMs, discovering offensive outputs and safety failures.
summary_zh: 使用LLM自动红队测试其他LLM，发现攻击性输出和安全故障。

experiment.key_results: Found 150+ unique failure modes via automated red teaming
experiment.real_world_impact: Influenced automated safety testing practices

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for LLMs to automatically red-team other LLMs, discovering offensive outputs and safety failures.
Applies Gopher, Chinchilla to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 150+ unique failure modes via automated red teaming.
Addresses toxic-output, data-leakage vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Not What You've Signed Up For: Compromising Real-World LLM-Integrated Applications with Indirect Prompt Injection
authors: Kai Greshake, Sahar Abdelnabi, Shailesh Mishra, Christoph Endres, Thorsten Holz, Mario Fritz
abstract: We demonstrate indirect prompt injection attacks on LLM-integrated applications. By planting adversarial instructions in external data sources (emails, web pages), attackers can manipulate LLM behavior when the application processes this data.
summary: Demonstrates indirect prompt injection via external data sources to compromise LLM-integrated applications.
summary_zh: 展示通过外部数据源的间接提示注入来攻破LLM集成应用。

experiment.key_results: Successfully compromised 5 real-world LLM-integrated applications
experiment.real_world_impact: Prompted security reviews of LLM-integrated products

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for demonstrates indirect prompt injection via external data sources to compromise LLM-integrated applications.
Applies GPT-4, GPT-3.5 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Successfully compromised 5 real-world LLM-integrated applications.
Addresses prompt-injection, data-exfiltration vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for demonstrates indirect prompt injection via external data sources to compromise LLM-integrated applications.
应用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Successfully compromised 5 real-world LLM-integrated applications.
解决 prompt-injection, data-exfiltration vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 展示通过外部数据源的间接提示注入来攻破LLM集成应用。
research_questions: What are the primary privacy risks associated with deploying LLMs in security-sensitive applications?
How effective are current defense mechanisms against privacy attacks on LLMs?
How can privacy-preserving techniques be applied without significantly degrading model performance?
research_questions_zh: 在安全敏感应用中部署LLM的主要隐私风险是什么？
当前防御机制对LLM隐私攻击的有效性如何？
如何在不显著降低模型性能的情况下应用隐私保护技术？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: Successfully compromised 5 real-world LLM-integrated applications.
The results represent a significant advancement in the field, establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
Further research is needed on scalable privacy-preserving techniques that maintain model utility.

研究成果代表了该领域的重要进展，建立了新的最先进基准。
需要进一步研究在保持模型效用的同时实现可扩展的隐私保护技术。

title: PromptGuard: Safe Prompt Engineering for LLM Applications
authors: Yi Liu, Gelei Jia, Neil Gong
abstract: We propose PromptGuard, a defense mechanism against prompt injection attacks in LLM applications. PromptGuard uses a combination of input sanitization, prompt isolation, and output validation to protect LLM applications from adversarial inputs.
summary: Proposes PromptGuard defense against prompt injection using input sanitization, isolation, and output validation.
summary_zh: 提出PromptGuard防御机制，通过输入净化、提示隔离和输出验证抵御提示注入攻击。

experiment.key_results: Blocks 95% of prompt injection attacks with 3% utility loss

contributions: Proposes PromptGuard, a novel approach for defense against prompt injection using input sanitization, isolation, and output validation.
Applies GPT-4, Claude to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on Prompt injection benchmark benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Blocks 95% of prompt injection attacks with 3% utility loss.
Addresses prompt-injection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 PromptGuard, a novel approach for defense against prompt injection using input sanitization, isolation, and output validation.
应用 GPT-4, Claude to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

title: Privacy Risks of Large Language Models: A Survey
authors: Haoran Li, Congying Song, Lingyu Xia
abstract: Comprehensive survey of privacy risks in LLMs covering training data extraction, membership inference, model inversion, privacy-preserving training, differential privacy, federated learning, and machine unlearning for LLMs.
summary: Comprehensive survey of LLM privacy risks covering data extraction, membership inference, and privacy-preserving methods.
summary_zh: LLM隐私风险的全面综述，涵盖数据提取、成员推断和隐私保护方法。

experiment.key_results: Reviews 200+ papers on LLM privacy risks and mitigations

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for comprehensive survey of LLM privacy risks covering data extraction, membership inference, and privacy-preserving methods.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Reviews 200+ papers on LLM privacy risks and mitigations.
Addresses privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.

解决 privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Differentially Private Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models
authors: Da Yu, Saurabh Naik, Arturs Backurs, Sivakanth Gopi, Huseyin A. Inan, Gautam Kamath, Janardhan Kulkarni, Yin Tat Lee, Andre Manoel, Lukas Wutschitz, Sergey Yekhanin, Huishuai Zhang
abstract: We study differentially private (DP) fine-tuning of LLMs, achieving strong privacy guarantees while maintaining model utility. We develop efficient DP-SGD variants that work with large models and demonstrate that DP fine-tuning can match non-private performance with moderate privacy budgets.
summary: Develops efficient differentially private fine-tuning for LLMs maintaining utility with strong privacy guarantees.
summary_zh: 开发高效的LLM差分隐私微调方法，在强隐私保证下保持模型效用。

experiment.key_results: DP fine-tuning matches non-private at epsilon=8, 95% utility retention

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for efficient differentially private fine-tuning for LLMs maintaining utility with strong privacy guarantees.
Applies GPT-2, Llama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on E2E, DART, WebNLG benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: DP fine-tuning matches non-private at epsilon=8, 95% utility retention.
Addresses privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for efficient differentially private fine-tuning for LLMs maintaining utility with strong privacy guarantees.
应用 GPT-2, Llama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
在以下数据集上进行评估： E2E, DART, WebNLG benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: DP fine-tuning matches non-private at epsilon=8, 95% utility retention.
解决 privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Membership Inference Attacks Against Large Language Models
authors: Michael Duan, Anshuman Suri, Niloofar Mireshghallah, Sewon Min, Weijia Shi, Luke Zettlemoyer, Yulia Tsvetkov, Yejin Choi, David Evans, Hannaneh Hajishirzi
abstract: Membership inference attacks determine whether specific data was used to train a model. We study MIA against LLMs, proposing new attack methods and evaluating defenses, showing that larger models are more susceptible.
summary: Studies membership inference attacks against LLMs, showing larger models are more susceptible.
summary_zh: 研究针对LLM的成员推断攻击，表明更大模型更易受攻击。

experiment.key_results: AUC of 0.85 for 13B model vs 0.62 for 125M model

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies membership inference attacks against LLMs, showing larger models are more susceptible.
Applies GPT-2, GPT-Neo, Pythia to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on WikiMIA benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: AUC of 0.85 for 13B model vs 0.62 for 125M model.
Addresses membership-inference vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies membership inference attacks against LLMs, showing larger models are more susceptible.
应用 GPT-2, GPT-Neo, Pythia to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

title: A Watermark for Large Language Models
authors: John Kirchenbauer, Jonas Geiping, Yuxin Wen, Jonathan Katz, Ian Miers, Tom Goldstein
abstract: We propose a watermarking scheme for LLM-generated text. By subtly biasing token selection during generation, our watermark is statistically detectable but imperceptible to humans, enabling attribution of AI-generated text.
summary: Proposes statistical watermarking for LLM text by biasing token selection, enabling AI text detection.
summary_zh: 提出通过偏置令牌选择的LLM文本统计水印，实现AI文本检测。

experiment.key_results: 99.9% detection rate at 0.1% false positive with 200+ tokens
experiment.real_world_impact: Widely adopted watermarking approach

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for statistical watermarking for LLM text by biasing token selection, enabling AI text detection.
Applies OPT, Llama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 2 baseline approaches including DetectGPT, GPTZero.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 99.9% detection rate at 0.1% false positive with 200+ tokens.
Addresses ai-detection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for statistical watermarking for LLM text by biasing token selection, enabling AI text detection.
应用 OPT, Llama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
进行了广泛的评估 against 2 baseline approaches including DetectGPT, GPTZero.
证明了 strong empirical results: 99.9% detection rate at 0.1% false positive with 200+ tokens.
解决 ai-detection vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Baseline Defenses for Adversarial Attacks Against Aligned Language Models
authors: Neel Jain, Avi Schwarzschild, Yuxin Wen, Gowthami Somepalli, John Kirchenbauer, Ping-yeh Chiang, Micah Goldblum, Aniruddha Saha, Jonas Geiping, Tom Goldstein
abstract: We evaluate simple baseline defenses against adversarial attacks on LLMs, including perplexity filtering, paraphrase defense, and retokenization. We find that simple defenses significantly reduce attack success rates.
summary: Evaluates simple baseline defenses (perplexity filtering, paraphrase, retokenization) against LLM adversarial attacks.
summary_zh: 评估简单的基线防御（困惑度过滤、改述、重新分词）对抗LLM对抗性攻击。

experiment.key_results: Perplexity filtering reduces GCG success from 99% to 2%

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for simple baseline defenses (perplexity filtering, paraphrase, retokenization) against LLM adversarial attacks.
Applies Llama-2, Vicuna to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on AdvBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Perplexity filtering reduces GCG success from 99% to 2%.
Addresses adversarial-attack vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for simple baseline defenses (perplexity filtering, paraphrase, retokenization) against LLM adversarial attacks.
应用 Llama-2, Vicuna to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

证明了 strong empirical results: Perplexity filtering reduces GCG success from 99% to 2%.
解决 adversarial-attack vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Rainbow Teaming: Open-Ended Generation of Diverse Adversarial Prompts
authors: Mikayel Li, Shrimai Prabhumoye, Mostofa Patwary, Roy Schwartz, Yulia Tsvetkov
abstract: We propose Rainbow Teaming for generating diverse adversarial prompts to comprehensively test LLM safety. Using quality-diversity optimization, Rainbow Teaming discovers a wide variety of failure modes that single-objective attacks miss.
summary: Proposes Rainbow Teaming using quality-diversity optimization for diverse adversarial prompt generation.
summary_zh: 提出Rainbow Teaming，使用质量多样性优化生成多样化对抗性提示。

experiment.key_results: Discovers 3x more diverse failure modes than GCG

contributions: Proposes Rainbow Teaming, a novel approach for using quality-diversity optimization for diverse adversarial prompt generation.
Applies Llama-2, GPT-3.5 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 3 baseline approaches including GCG, PAIR, AutoDAN.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Discovers 3x more diverse failure modes than GCG.
Addresses jailbreak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 Rainbow Teaming, a novel approach for using quality-diversity optimization for diverse adversarial prompt generation.
应用 Llama-2, GPT-3.5 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
进行了广泛的评估 against 3 baseline approaches including GCG, PAIR, AutoDAN.
证明了 strong empirical results: Discovers 3x more diverse failure modes than GCG.
解决 jailbreak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Poisoning Large Language Models via Instruction Tuning
authors: Yangsibo Huang, Samyak Gupta, Mengzhou Zhong, Kai Li, Danqi Chen
abstract: We study data poisoning attacks on instruction-tuned LLMs. By injecting a small number of poisoned instruction-response pairs, an attacker can cause the model to produce targeted harmful outputs when triggered by specific inputs.
summary: Studies data poisoning attacks on instruction-tuned LLMs through injecting poisoned training pairs.
summary_zh: 研究通过注入中毒训练对来攻击指令调优LLM的数据投毒攻击。

experiment.key_results: 100 poisoned samples (0.2%) achieve 90%+ attack success rate

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies data poisoning attacks on instruction-tuned LLMs through injecting poisoned training pairs.
Applies Llama-2, Alpaca to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on Alpaca dataset benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 100 poisoned samples (0.2%) achieve 90%+ attack success rate.
Addresses data-poisoning, backdoor vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies data poisoning attacks on instruction-tuned LLMs through injecting poisoned training pairs.
应用 Llama-2, Alpaca to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

title: Scalable Extraction of Training Data from (Production) Language Models
authors: Milad Nasr, Nicholas Carlini, Jonathan Hayase, Matthew Jagielski, A. Feder Cooper, Daphne Ippolito, Christopher A. Choquette-Choo, Eric Wallace, Florian Tramer, Katherine Lee
abstract: We demonstrate scalable extraction of training data from production LLMs including ChatGPT. Using a novel divergence attack, we extract gigabytes of training data from aligned models, showing that alignment does not prevent data extraction.
summary: Demonstrates scalable training data extraction from ChatGPT using divergence attacks, showing alignment doesn't prevent extraction.
summary_zh: 展示使用发散攻击从ChatGPT大规模提取训练数据，表明对齐不能防止数据提取。

experiment.key_results: Extracted several gigabytes of training data from ChatGPT at $200 cost
experiment.real_world_impact: Led OpenAI to patch the specific vulnerability

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for demonstrates scalable training data extraction from ChatGPT using divergence attacks, showing alignment doesn't prevent.
Applies ChatGPT, GPT-3.5 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Extracted several gigabytes of training data from ChatGPT at $200 cost.
Addresses data-extraction vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: HarmBench: A Standardized Evaluation Framework for Automated Red Teaming and Robust Refusal
authors: Mantas Mazeika, Long Phan, Xuwang Yin, Andy Zou, Zifan Wang, Norman Mu, Elham Sakaguchi, Sheng Li, Steven Basart, Bo Li, David Forsyth, Dan Hendrycks
abstract: We present HarmBench, a standardized framework for evaluating automated red teaming attacks and LLM defenses. HarmBench includes 510 harmful behaviors across 7 categories with standardized evaluation using a fine-tuned classifier.
summary: Introduces HarmBench, standardized framework with 510 harmful behaviors for evaluating LLM attacks and defenses.
summary_zh: 引入HarmBench，包含510种有害行为的标准化框架，用于评估LLM攻击和防御。

experiment.key_results: No single defense achieves <10% ASR across all attack types
experiment.real_world_impact: Widely adopted benchmark for LLM safety

Applies GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on HarmBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: No single defense achieves <10% ASR across all attack types.
Addresses jailbreak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
authors: Alexander Wei, Nika Haghtalab, Jacob Steinhardt
abstract: We categorize why LLM safety training fails, identifying two fundamental failure modes: competing objectives (where helpfulness conflicts with safety) and mismatched generalization (where safety training doesn't generalize to novel attacks).
summary: Categorizes LLM safety failures into competing objectives and mismatched generalization failure modes.
summary_zh: 将LLM安全失败分为竞争目标和泛化不匹配两种失败模式。

experiment.key_results: Identifies 2 fundamental failure modes explaining most jailbreak successes

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for categorizes LLM safety failures into competing objectives and mismatched generalization failure modes.
Applies GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Identifies 2 fundamental failure modes explaining most jailbreak successes.
Addresses jailbreak, safety-failure vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

应用 GPT-4, Claude, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
提供了实证评估 demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
证明了 strong empirical results: Identifies 2 fundamental failure modes explaining most jailbreak successes.
解决 jailbreak, safety-failure vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Jailbreaking Black-Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
authors: Patrick Chao, Alexander Robey, Edgar Dobriban, Hamed Hassani, George J. Pappas, Eric Wong
abstract: We introduce PAIR (Prompt Automatic Iterative Refinement), an algorithm that uses an attacker LLM to iteratively refine jailbreak prompts against a target LLM. PAIR requires only black-box access and typically succeeds within 20 queries.
summary: PAIR algorithm uses attacker LLM to iteratively refine jailbreaks against target LLMs with only black-box access.
summary_zh: PAIR算法使用攻击者LLM迭代优化针对目标LLM的越狱提示，仅需黑盒访问。

experiment.key_results: 60% success on GPT-4 in <20 queries

contributions: Proposes PAIR, a novel approach for algorithm uses attacker LLM to iteratively refine jailbreaks against target LLMs with only black-box access.
Applies GPT-4, Claude, Vicuna to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on AdvBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 60% success on GPT-4 in <20 queries.
Addresses jailbreak vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Machine Unlearning for Large Language Models: A Survey
authors: Yian Wu, Guoliang Pang, Yang Liu
abstract: Machine unlearning enables removing specific knowledge from trained models. This survey reviews unlearning techniques for LLMs, covering exact unlearning, approximate methods, and evaluation metrics for verifying knowledge removal.
summary: Survey of machine unlearning techniques for LLMs covering exact, approximate methods, and verification.
summary_zh: LLM机器遗忘技术综述，涵盖精确方法、近似方法和验证指标。

experiment.key_results: Reviews 50+ unlearning methods, identifies key challenges for LLM unlearning

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for survey of machine unlearning techniques for LLMs covering exact, approximate methods, and verification.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Reviews 50+ unlearning methods, identifies key challenges for LLM unlearning.
Addresses privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Red-Teaming LLM Watermarks
authors: Zhen Shi, Yihan Chen, Yuxin Wen
abstract: We red-team existing LLM watermarking schemes, demonstrating attacks that remove watermarks while preserving text quality. Our attacks include paraphrasing, token substitution, and recursive editing that defeat current watermarking approaches.
summary: Red-teams LLM watermarks showing attacks that remove watermarks via paraphrasing and editing.
summary_zh: 红队测试LLM水印，展示通过改述和编辑去除水印的攻击。

experiment.key_results: Reduces watermark detection from 99% to 15% while preserving 90% text quality

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for red-teams LLM watermarks showing attacks that remove watermarks via paraphrasing and editing.
Applies GPT-4, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 2 baseline approaches including KGW watermark, SWEET watermark.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Reduces watermark detection from 99% to 15% while preserving 90% text quality.
Addresses watermark-removal vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Federated Learning for Large Language Models: Challenges and Solutions
authors: Tian Li, Anit Kumar Sahu, Manzil Zaheer, Maziar Sanjabi, Ameet Talwalkar, Virginia Smith
abstract: Federated learning enables collaborative LLM training without sharing raw data. We address key challenges in federated LLM training including communication efficiency, heterogeneity, and privacy guarantees.
summary: Addresses challenges in federated LLM training: communication efficiency, heterogeneity, and privacy.
summary_zh: 解决联邦LLM训练中的挑战：通信效率、异质性和隐私保证。

experiment.key_results: Federated fine-tuning achieves 95% of centralized performance

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for addresses challenges in federated LLM training: communication efficiency, heterogeneity, and privacy.
Applies Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on Natural Instructions benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Federated fine-tuning achieves 95% of centralized performance.
Addresses privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Trustworthy LLMs: A Survey and Guideline for Evaluating Large Language Models' Alignment
authors: Yang Liu, Yuanshun Yao, Jean-Francois Ton, Xiaoying Zhang, Ruocheng Cheng, Yegor Klochkov, Muhammad Faaiz Taufiq, Hang Li
abstract: We survey trustworthiness dimensions of LLMs including truthfulness, safety, fairness, robustness, privacy, machine ethics, and transparency. We provide guidelines for evaluating each dimension and identify gaps.
summary: Surveys LLM trustworthiness across 7 dimensions including safety, fairness, robustness, and privacy.

experiment.key_results: Provides evaluation framework across 7 trust dimensions

Demonstrates strong empirical results: Provides evaluation framework across 7 trust dimensions.

title: Copyright Protection for LLM-Generated Code
authors: Haoxiang Wu, Zhengran Chen, Hao Sun
abstract: LLMs can generate code that infringes copyright by reproducing training data. We study copyright risks in LLM code generation and propose techniques to detect and mitigate copyright-infringing code outputs.
summary: Studies copyright risks in LLM code generation and proposes detection and mitigation techniques.
summary_zh: 研究LLM代码生成中的版权风险，提出检测和缓解技术。

experiment.key_results: 5-10% of generated code has substantial similarity to training data

contributions: Proposes a approach for studies copyright risks in LLM code generation and proposes detection and mitigation techniques.
Applies GPT-4, Copilot, CodeLlama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on GitHub licensed repos benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 5-10% of generated code has substantial similarity to training data.
Addresses copyright vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a approach for studies copyright risks in LLM code generation and proposes detection and mitigation techniques.
应用 GPT-4, Copilot, CodeLlama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

解决 copyright vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Confidential Computing for Large Language Model Inference
authors: Chenghao Wang, Bo Li, Zheng Zhang
abstract: Running LLM inference in untrusted environments risks exposing user prompts and model weights. We propose using confidential computing (TEEs) for LLM inference, addressing performance challenges through optimized enclave design.
summary: Uses confidential computing (TEEs) for privacy-preserving LLM inference in untrusted environments.
summary_zh: 使用机密计算（TEE）在不可信环境中实现隐私保护的LLM推理。

experiment.key_results: Only 15% inference overhead for 7B model in TEE

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for confidential computing (TEEs) for privacy-preserving LLM inference in untrusted environments.
Applies Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 1 baseline approaches including Unprotected inference.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Only 15% inference overhead for 7B model in TEE.
Addresses privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

应用 Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

证明了 strong empirical results: Only 15% inference overhead for 7B model in TEE.
解决 privacy vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: LLM Guardrails: A Comprehensive Survey of Safety Mechanisms
authors: Jiaxin Chen, Yue Wang, Hang Li
abstract: We survey guardrail mechanisms for LLMs including input/output filters, constitutional AI, RLHF, red teaming, and runtime monitoring. We compare approaches on effectiveness, cost, and impact on model utility.

title: Stealing Large Language Model Parameters via Side-Channel Attacks
authors: Yixin Zhao, Guang Chen, Haoyu Wang
abstract: We demonstrate side-channel attacks that extract LLM parameters from GPU memory access patterns. By monitoring cache timing and memory access patterns during inference, we reconstruct model weights with high fidelity.
summary: Demonstrates GPU side-channel attacks extracting LLM parameters from memory access patterns.
summary_zh: 展示GPU侧信道攻击从内存访问模式中提取LLM参数。

experiment.key_results: Reconstructs 70% of model weights from GPU cache timing

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for demonstrates GPU side-channel attacks extracting LLM parameters from memory access patterns.
Applies GPT-2, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Reconstructs 70% of model weights from GPU cache timing.
Addresses model-stealing, side-channel vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

应用 GPT-2, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

证明了 strong empirical results: Reconstructs 70% of model weights from GPU cache timing.
解决 model-stealing, side-channel vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: PII Detection and Redaction in LLM Outputs
authors: Yuxin Wan, Sen Chen, Bo Li
abstract: LLMs may inadvertently output personally identifiable information (PII). We develop methods for real-time PII detection and redaction in LLM outputs, using a lightweight classifier to filter sensitive information before it reaches users.
summary: Develops real-time PII detection and redaction for LLM outputs using lightweight classifiers.
summary_zh: 开发使用轻量级分类器对LLM输出进行实时PII检测和编辑的方法。

experiment.key_results: 97% PII recall with 1ms additional latency per token

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for real-time PII detection and redaction for LLM outputs using lightweight classifiers.
Applies GPT-3.5, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on PII-Bench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 97% PII recall with 1ms additional latency per token.
Addresses pii-leakage vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for real-time PII detection and redaction for LLM outputs using lightweight classifiers.
应用 GPT-3.5, Llama-2 to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

title: Bias and Fairness in Large Language Models: A Survey
authors: Emily Zhang, Wenjie Yin, Yun Chen
abstract: We survey bias and fairness issues in LLMs, covering types of bias (gender, racial, cultural), measurement methodologies, mitigation strategies, and regulatory implications for LLM deployment.
summary: Surveys LLM bias and fairness covering types, measurement, mitigation, and regulatory implications.
summary_zh: 调查LLM偏见和公平性问题，涵盖类型、测量、缓解和监管影响。

experiment.key_results: Reviews 100+ papers, proposes unified fairness evaluation framework

Provides empirical evaluation demonstrating significant improvements over prior approaches.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Reviews 100+ papers, proposes unified fairness evaluation framework.
Addresses bias vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Securing LLM APIs: Authentication, Rate Limiting, and Abuse Prevention
authors: Yifan Huang, Neil Gong, Peng Liu
abstract: LLM APIs face unique security challenges including prompt extraction, model misuse, and cost attacks. We study API-level security measures and propose enhanced authentication and abuse prevention mechanisms for LLM service providers.
summary: Studies LLM API security including prompt extraction, misuse, and cost attacks, proposing enhanced protection.
summary_zh: 研究LLM API安全性，包括提示提取、误用和成本攻击，提出增强保护机制。

experiment.key_results: Demonstrates 3 novel API attack vectors, proposes mitigations

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies LLM API security including prompt extraction, misuse, and cost attacks, proposing enhanced protection.

Demonstrates strong empirical results: Demonstrates 3 novel API attack vectors, proposes mitigations.

title: Attacking LLM Agents: Prompt Injection in Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Yupei Liu, Jinyuan Jia, Neil Gong
abstract: Multi-agent LLM systems introduce new attack surfaces. We demonstrate how compromising a single agent can cascade to manipulate the entire multi-agent system through inter-agent communication, achieving goal hijacking and data exfiltration.
summary: Demonstrates cascading attacks in multi-agent LLM systems through inter-agent prompt injection.
summary_zh: 展示多代理LLM系统中通过代理间提示注入的级联攻击。

experiment.key_results: Successfully compromises 8/10 multi-agent systems via single agent

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for demonstrates cascading attacks in multi-agent LLM systems through inter-agent prompt injection.
Applies GPT-4, Claude to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Conducts extensive evaluation against 2 baseline approaches including Direct injection, No attack.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Successfully compromises 8/10 multi-agent systems via single agent.
Addresses prompt-injection, goal-hijacking vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

应用 GPT-4, Claude to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

title: Security Implications of LLM Hallucinations in Code Generation
authors: Yifan Chen, Neil Thompson, Zhen Li
abstract: LLM hallucinations in code generation can introduce security vulnerabilities. We study how hallucinated package names, API calls, and code patterns create supply chain and logic vulnerabilities in LLM-generated code.
summary: Studies security implications of LLM code hallucinations creating supply chain and logic vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 研究LLM代码幻觉创建供应链和逻辑漏洞的安全影响。

experiment.key_results: 3.5% of generated code references non-existent packages (squattable)
experiment.real_world_impact: Led to new package squatting awareness

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies security implications of LLM code hallucinations creating supply chain and logic vulnerabilities.
Applies GPT-4, Copilot, CodeLlama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.
Evaluates on HumanEval, CodeGen benchmarks benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 3.5% of generated code references non-existent packages (squattable).
Addresses hallucinated-package, api-misuse vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies security implications of LLM code hallucinations creating supply chain and logic vulnerabilities.
应用 GPT-4, Copilot, CodeLlama to analyze and address security and privacy concerns in software systems.

证明了 strong empirical results: 3.5% of generated code references non-existent packages (squattable).
解决 hallucinated-package, api-misuse vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究LLM代码幻觉创建供应链和逻辑漏洞的安全影响。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?

什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？

title: Boosting Fuzzer Efficiency: An Information Theoretic Approach with LLMs
authors: Marcel Bohme, Van-Thuan Pham, Abhik Roychoudhury
abstract: We propose using LLMs to estimate information content of fuzzing inputs, prioritizing mutations that are likely to increase coverage. This information-theoretic approach guides the fuzzer toward unexplored program behaviors more efficiently.
summary: Uses LLMs for information-theoretic guidance of fuzzing, prioritizing mutations likely to increase coverage.
summary_zh: 利用LLM进行模糊测试的信息论引导，优先选择可能增加覆盖率的变异。

experiment.key_results: 20% more coverage in same time budget vs AFL++

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs for information-theoretic guidance of fuzzing, prioritizing mutations likely to increase coverage.
Leverages GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 20% more coverage in same time budget vs AFL++.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs for information-theoretic guidance of fuzzing, prioritizing mutations likely to increase coverage.
利用 GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： FuzzBench benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 20% more coverage in same time budget vs AFL++.
解决 crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 利用LLM进行模糊测试的信息论引导，优先选择可能增加覆盖率的变异。
research_questions: How can LLMs improve fuzzing effectiveness in terms of code coverage and bug discovery?
What is the optimal integration strategy for combining LLM capabilities with traditional fuzzing techniques?
What types of bugs can LLM-enhanced fuzzing discover that conventional fuzzers miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM如何在代码覆盖率和漏洞发现方面提高模糊测试效果？
将LLM能力与传统模糊测试技术结合的最优集成策略是什么？
LLM增强的模糊测试能发现哪些传统模糊器遗漏的漏洞类型？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 20% more coverage in same time budget vs AFL++.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：20% more coverage in same time budget vs AFL++。
研究发现为从事LLM安全工具研究的研究人员和从业者提供了实用见解。
未来工作应探索LLM推理与覆盖率反馈的更紧密集成。

title: DeepFuzz: Deep Learning Augmented Fuzzing
authors: Wei Ye, Ravi Shin, Dawn Song
abstract: We present DeepFuzz, combining deep learning-based input generation with traditional coverage-guided fuzzing. DeepFuzz uses a neural network to learn input structure and generate smart mutations that maintain validity while exploring new paths.
summary: Combines deep learning input generation with coverage-guided fuzzing for smart structure-aware mutations.
summary_zh: 结合深度学习输入生成与覆盖引导模糊测试，实现智能结构感知变异。

experiment.key_results: Found 28 new bugs in mature parsers

contributions: Proposes DeepFuzz, a novel approach for combines deep learning input generation with coverage-guided fuzzing for smart structure-aware mutations.
Leverages Transformer (custom) for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on XML parsers, JSON parsers benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Found 28 new bugs in mature parsers.
Addresses crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: NeuronFuzz: Neural Network Guided Greybox Fuzzing
authors: Qingchao Shen, Haonan Chen, Yuekang Li
abstract: NeuronFuzz uses neural networks to predict which mutations will increase code coverage. By training on execution traces and coverage feedback, the neural model learns to select promising mutation locations and strategies.
summary: Uses neural networks to predict coverage-increasing mutations, learning from execution traces and feedback.
summary_zh: 使用神经网络预测增加覆盖率的变异，从执行轨迹和反馈中学习。

experiment.key_results: 15% more branches covered in 24h vs AFL++

contributions: Proposes NeuronFuzz, a novel approach for neural networks to predict coverage-increasing mutations, learning from execution traces and feedback.
Leverages Custom transformer for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.

Demonstrates strong empirical results: 15% more branches covered in 24h vs AFL++.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: LLM-Enhanced Smart Fuzzing for Network Protocols
authors: Tao Liu, Yue Wang, Jun Chen
abstract: Network protocol fuzzing requires valid message sequences. We use LLMs to understand protocol state machines from RFCs and generate smart fuzzing strategies that exercise protocol edge cases.
summary: Uses LLMs to understand protocol state machines from RFCs for smart protocol fuzzing.
summary_zh: 利用LLM从RFC理解协议状态机，实现智能协议模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: 35% more state transitions explored than AFLNet

Demonstrates strong empirical results: 35% more state transitions explored than AFLNet.
Addresses crash, memory-corruption vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: APIFuzz: LLM-Guided API Sequence Generation for Library Fuzzing
authors: Zhengjie Wang, Shuai Li, Chao Zhang
abstract: Library fuzzing requires valid API call sequences. APIFuzz uses LLMs to understand library API semantics and generate valid, diverse API call sequences that exercise complex library functionality.
summary: Uses LLMs to generate valid API call sequences for library fuzzing based on semantic understanding.
summary_zh: 利用LLM基于语义理解生成有效的API调用序列，用于库模糊测试。

experiment.key_results: 42% more API coverage than FUDGE

contributions: Proposes APIFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to generate valid API call sequences for library fuzzing based on semantic understanding.
Leverages GPT-4, CodeLlama for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on 10 libraries benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 42% more API coverage than FUDGE.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 APIFuzz, a novel approach for LLMs to generate valid API call sequences for library fuzzing based on semantic understanding.

Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.

未来工作应探索LLM推理与覆盖率反馈的更紧密集成。

title: Adaptive LLM-Guided Fuzzing with Dynamic Strategy Selection
authors: Yong Chen, Lei Wu, Marcel Bohme
abstract: Different fuzzing targets benefit from different strategies. We propose adaptive fuzzing that uses LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on observed coverage patterns, program structure, and historical effectiveness.
summary: Adaptive fuzzing using LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on coverage and program structure.
summary_zh: 自适应模糊测试，利用LLM根据覆盖率和程序结构动态选择变异策略。

experiment.key_results: 18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for adaptive fuzzing using LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on coverage and program structure.
Leverages GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
Evaluates on Magma, FuzzBench benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++.
Addresses crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for adaptive fuzzing using LLMs to dynamically select mutation strategies based on coverage and program structure.
利用 GPT-3.5 for intelligent test case generation and mutation strategies.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Magma, FuzzBench benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++.
解决 crash, memory-safety vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 自适应模糊测试，利用LLM根据覆盖率和程序结构动态选择变异策略。
research_questions: How can LLMs improve fuzzing effectiveness in terms of code coverage and bug discovery?
What is the optimal integration strategy for combining LLM capabilities with traditional fuzzing techniques?
What types of bugs can LLM-enhanced fuzzing discover that conventional fuzzers miss?
research_questions_zh: LLM如何在代码覆盖率和漏洞发现方面提高模糊测试效果？
将LLM能力与传统模糊测试技术结合的最优集成策略是什么？
LLM增强的模糊测试能发现哪些传统模糊器遗漏的漏洞类型？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should explore tighter integration of LLM reasoning with coverage feedback for more effective bug discovery.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：18% faster bug finding than static strategy AFL++。

title: Can LLMs Write Secure Code? A Large-Scale Experiment with ChatGPT
authors: Shafiq Ullah, Yu Han, Yang Liu
abstract: We study whether LLM-generated code is secure by analyzing 4000 code samples from ChatGPT across 10 languages. We find that 25% of generated code contains security vulnerabilities, with injection flaws being most common.
summary: Studies security of LLM-generated code across 10 languages, finding 25% contains vulnerabilities.
summary_zh: 研究10种语言中LLM生成代码的安全性，发现25%包含漏洞。

experiment.key_results: 25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for studies security of LLM-generated code across 10 languages, finding 25% contains vulnerabilities.
Leverages ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on 4000 generated samples benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code.
Addresses injection, XSS, path-traversal vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for studies security of LLM-generated code across 10 languages, finding 25% contains vulnerabilities.
利用 ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 4000 generated samples benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: 25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code.
解决 injection, XSS, path-traversal vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 研究10种语言中LLM生成代码的安全性，发现25%包含漏洞。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
How do different LLMs (ChatGPT, GPT-4, Copilot) compare in vulnerability detection accuracy and coverage?
What prompting or fine-tuning strategies maximize vulnerability detection performance?
research_questions_zh: LLM在漏洞检测方面与传统静态分析工具相比效果如何？
什么提示或微调策略能最大化漏洞检测性能？
当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: 25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code.
The findings provide practical insights for researchers and practitioners working on LLM-based security tools.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.
conclusions_zh: 实验评估展示了显著的成果：25% of LLM code has vulnerabilities, vs 15% for human code。
研究发现为从事LLM安全工具研究的研究人员和从业者提供了实用见解。
未来工作应着重于减少误报并扩展到更多样化的代码库和漏洞类型。

title: SecurityEval: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Code Security
authors: Tony Zhuo, Yifan Huang, Zheng Chen
abstract: We present SecurityEval, a benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the security of LLM-generated code. SecurityEval contains 500 prompts that are likely to produce vulnerable code, covering 30 CWE categories across 5 languages.
summary: Introduces SecurityEval benchmark with 500 prompts evaluating LLM code security across 30 CWEs.
summary_zh: 引入SecurityEval基准，包含500个提示评估LLM代码安全性，涵盖30种CWE。

experiment.key_results: GPT-4 produces least vulnerable code but still 18% vulnerability rate

contributions: Proposes SecurityEval, a novel approach for benchmark with 500 prompts evaluating LLM code security across 30 CWEs.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on SecurityEval benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: GPT-4 produces least vulnerable code but still 18% vulnerability rate.
Addresses 30 CWE categories vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 SecurityEval, a novel approach for benchmark with 500 prompts evaluating LLM code security across 30 CWEs.
利用 GPT-4, Claude, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

解决 30 CWE categories vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?
How do different LLMs (GPT-4, Claude, CodeLlama) compare in vulnerability detection accuracy and coverage?

title: Do Users Write More Insecure Code with AI Assistants?
authors: Neil Perry, Megha Srivastava, Deepak Kumar, Dan Boneh
abstract: We conduct a controlled user study to evaluate whether AI code assistants lead to less secure code. Participants with AI assistance produced significantly more security vulnerabilities than those without, while believing their code was more secure.
summary: User study showing AI code assistants lead to more security vulnerabilities while users believe code is more secure.
summary_zh: 用户研究表明AI代码助手导致更多安全漏洞，而用户认为代码更安全。

experiment.key_results: AI-assisted participants wrote 10% more vulnerable code
experiment.real_world_impact: Influenced AI assistant safety discussions

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for user study showing AI code assistants lead to more security vulnerabilities while users believe code is more secure.
Leverages Codex for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on 5 programming tasks benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: AI-assisted participants wrote 10% more vulnerable code.
Addresses SQL-injection, path-traversal, XSS vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for user study showing AI code assistants lead to more security vulnerabilities while users believe code is more secure.
利用 Codex for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 5 programming tasks benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: AI-assisted participants wrote 10% more vulnerable code.
解决 SQL-injection, path-traversal, XSS vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
abstract_zh: 用户研究表明AI代码助手导致更多安全漏洞，而用户认为代码更安全。
research_questions: How effectively can LLMs detect software vulnerabilities compared to traditional static analysis tools?

当前LLM漏洞检测方法的主要局限性和失败模式是什么？
conclusions: The experimental evaluation demonstrates significant results: AI-assisted participants wrote 10% more vulnerable code.
The results represent a significant advancement in the field, establishing new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
Future work should focus on reducing false positives and expanding to more diverse codebases and vulnerability types.

title: Large Language Models for Software Vulnerability Detection: A Comprehensive Benchmark Study
authors: Xin He, Jiayuan Bai, Lin Chen
abstract: We benchmark 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection across multiple datasets and evaluate various prompting techniques. Our study reveals that few-shot prompting with examples significantly improves detection performance.
summary: Benchmarks 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection with various prompting techniques, finding few-shot best.
summary_zh: 对10个LLM在多种提示技术下进行漏洞检测基准测试，发现少样本最佳。

experiment.key_results: Few-shot improves F1 by 15% over zero-shot across all models

contributions: Introduces a novel approach for benchmarks 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection with various prompting techniques, finding few-shot best.
Leverages GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Big-Vul, Devign, SARD benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Few-shot improves F1 by 15% over zero-shot across all models.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 引入 a novel approach for benchmarks 10 LLMs for vulnerability detection with various prompting techniques, finding few-shot best.
利用 GPT-4, GPT-3.5, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: VulGuard: Real-Time LLM-Based Vulnerability Detection in CI/CD
authors: Chen Li, Yue Wang, Tao Zhang
abstract: Integrating vulnerability detection into CI/CD requires balancing speed and accuracy. VulGuard uses lightweight LLM inference with incremental analysis to detect vulnerabilities in code changes in real-time, focusing on modified code rather than full codebase.
summary: Real-time LLM vulnerability detection in CI/CD using incremental analysis of code changes.
summary_zh: 使用增量分析代码变更在CI/CD中进行实时LLM漏洞检测。

experiment.key_results: <30s per PR review, detects 65% of vulnerabilities in changed code

contributions: Proposes VulGuard, a novel approach for real-time LLM vulnerability detection in CI/CD using incremental analysis of code changes.
Leverages GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on Real GitHub PRs benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: <30s per PR review, detects 65% of vulnerabilities in changed code.
Addresses injection, auth, crypto vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 VulGuard, a novel approach for real-time LLM vulnerability detection in CI/CD using incremental analysis of code changes.
利用 GPT-3.5, CodeLlama for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： Real GitHub PRs benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
证明了 strong empirical results: <30s per PR review, detects 65% of vulnerabilities in changed code.
解决 injection, auth, crypto vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.

title: Repository-Level Vulnerability Detection with LLMs
authors: Ziyang Wang, Hao Li, Dawn Song
abstract: Most LLM vulnerability detection focuses on function-level analysis. We extend to repository-level detection, where the LLM analyzes cross-file data flows, configuration files, and architectural patterns to find vulnerabilities that span multiple files.
summary: Extends LLM vulnerability detection to repository-level, analyzing cross-file data flows and architectural patterns.
summary_zh: 将LLM漏洞检测扩展到仓库级别，分析跨文件数据流和架构模式。

experiment.key_results: Finds 40% more vulnerabilities than function-level analysis

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for extends LLM vulnerability detection to repository-level, analyzing cross-file data flows and architectural patterns.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on 20 OSS repositories benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: Finds 40% more vulnerabilities than function-level analysis.
Addresses cross-file-injection, config-vuln, auth-bypass vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for extends LLM vulnerability detection to repository-level, analyzing cross-file data flows and architectural patterns.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
在以下数据集上进行评估： 20 OSS repositories benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.

title: Not Just Detection: LLMs for Vulnerability Explanation and Remediation
authors: Chengcheng Sun, Zhengzi Xu, Chao Zhang
abstract: Beyond detection, developers need vulnerability explanations and fix suggestions. We evaluate LLMs' ability to explain detected vulnerabilities in natural language and suggest correct remediations, making security findings more actionable.
summary: Evaluates LLMs for vulnerability explanation and remediation suggestion, making security findings actionable.
summary_zh: 评估LLM的漏洞解释和修复建议能力，使安全发现可操作。

experiment.key_results: 85% of explanations rated correct by security experts

contributions: Proposes a novel approach for LLMs for vulnerability explanation and remediation suggestion, making security findings actionable.
Leverages GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on CVE database benchmark, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 85% of explanations rated correct by security experts.
Addresses various vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
contributions_zh: 提出 a novel approach for LLMs for vulnerability explanation and remediation suggestion, making security findings actionable.
利用 GPT-4, Claude for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.

title: VulnCoder: Fine-Tuning Code LLMs for Vulnerability Detection
authors: Shengjian Ma, Yizheng Chen, David Wagner
abstract: We present VulnCoder, a series of code LLMs fine-tuned specifically for vulnerability detection. Through careful data curation, training strategy design, and evaluation, VulnCoder achieves SOTA on multiple vulnerability benchmarks.
summary: Fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for vulnerability detection, achieving SOTA on multiple benchmarks.
summary_zh: 专门针对漏洞检测微调代码LLM，在多个基准上达到最先进水平。

experiment.key_results: 33B model achieves 82% F1, surpassing GPT-4 zero-shot by 10%

contributions: Proposes VulnCoder, a novel approach for fine-tunes code LLMs specifically for vulnerability detection, achieving SOTA on multiple benchmarks.
Leverages CodeLlama, DeepSeek-Coder for automated vulnerability analysis and detection.
Evaluates on DiverseVul, Big-Vul, CVEFixes benchmarks, providing comprehensive empirical evidence.
Demonstrates strong empirical results: 33B model achieves 82% F1, surpassing GPT-4 zero-shot by 10%.
Addresses CWE-119, CWE-20, CWE-416 vulnerability types, expanding coverage of security analysis.
